Title: 훈규 님 차례입니다. 🏆
Body: 회사 동료 7,118명이 이미 LinkedIn 퍼즐 게임을 해결했습니다. LinkedIn 게임은 재충전과 집중을 돕기 위해 짧은 퍼즐을 매일 제공합니다. 30초 휴식! - Zip 점들을 순서대로 연결해서 그리드를 얼마나 빨리 채울 수 있는지 확인해 보세요. 플레이: https://www.linkedin.com/comm/games/zip?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_games_discovery_social%3BuvVAN%2FZJQzuURa1Q3e%2F8kQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQEvm-F-ggEHAg&midSig=1PKNszzF9o_Yc1&trk=eml-email_games_discovery_social-null-0-null&trkEmail=eml-email_games_discovery_social-null-0-null-null-mlvpjy~mohq15a3~eu-null-null&eid=mlvpjy-mohq15a3-eu 3분 휴식! Title: [라프텔] 서비스 이용약관 개정 안내
Body:
라프텔 서비스 이용약관 개정 안내

안녕하세요. 라프텔입니다.

항상 저희 라프텔을 믿고 이용해 주시는 고객님께 진심으로 감사드립니다.
라프텔의 서비스 이용약관을 개정하게 되어 아래와 같이 사전 안내드리오니 서비스 이용에 참고 바랍니다.

개정 사유

  • 라프텔 스토어 주문 취소 및 정정에 관한 기준 명확화

  • 라프텔 스토어 주문 취소 및 정정 관련 조항 신설에 따른 일부 조문의 번호 변경

개정 사항

현재 내용

개정 내용

관련 조항 없음제18조 주문의 취소 및 정정
1. 회사는 몰에 표시된 상품의 가격 및 정보의 정확성을 유지하기 위하여 노력합니다. 다만, 전산 오류, 입력 착오, 시스템 장애 등으로 인하여 상품의 가격 또는 정보가 정상적인 범위와 다르게 표시될 수 있습니다.
2. 회원의 주문이 접수된 이후라도 제1항에 따른 가격 또는 정보의 오류가 발생한 경우, 회사는 해당 주문에 대하여 계약의 성립을 거절하거나 취소할 수 있습니다.
3. 회사는 제2항에 따라 주문을 취소하는 경우 지체 없이 해당 상품의 판매를 중단하고, 제5조에 정한 방법으로 회원에게 그 사유를 통지합니다.
4. 회사는 제2항에 따라 주문이 취소된 경우 관련 법령에 따라 지체 없이 환불에 필요한 조치를 하며, 회원이 결제한 금액을 원결제수단으로 환불하거나 환불에 필요한 조치를 합니다. 다만, 결제수단별 사업자의 사정에 따라 실제 환불 완료 시점은 상이할 수 있습니다.
5. 본 조에 따른 주문취소는 「민법」 제109조 및 「전자상거래 등에서의 소비자보호에 관한 법률」 등 관련 법령에 따릅니다.

개정 일정

  • 공고 일자 : 2026년 4월 20일

  • 시행 일자 : 2026년 6월 8일

개정된 약관은 시행일부터 자동으로 적용됩니다. 본 개정에 동의하지 않으실 경우, 회원 탈퇴(거부 의사 표시)를 요청하실 수 있으며, 시행일 이전까지 거부 의사를 표시하지 않으실 경우 개정에 동의하신 것으로 간주됩니다.

라프텔 이용약관 확인하기

본 메일은 발신 전용이므로 회신이 불가합니다.

궁금한 점이 있거나 도움이 필요하시다면 고객센터로 문의해 주시기 바랍니다.

앞으로도 보다 나은 서비스 제공을 위해 최선을 다하겠습니다.

주식회사 라프텔 / 대표 박종원
서울특별시 영등포구 국제금융로 10, 13층 라프텔(여의도동, 서울국제 금융센터 투아이에프씨)
사업자등록번호 535-86-02250 / 통신판매업신고 제 2022-서울영등포-3578호

Title: 어드벤처 - [1Day] 삼성카드 제휴
Body: 안훈규 고객님 롯데월드 어드벤처 [1Day] 삼성카드 제휴(1매) 예매가 완료되었습니다. 예매내역 상세정보 예매상품 [1Day] 삼성카드 제휴 예매구분 온라인예매 결제정보 제휴카드 예매상태 예매완료 예매일자 2026-04-23 (목요일) 15:02:42 방문일자 2026-04-24 (금요일) 예매번호 [총 1매] 8195-3213-2274-2895 결제금액 [총 32,500 원] - 어른 x 1 = 32,500원 쿠폰할인 0 원 포인트사용 0 포인트 예매자 정보 예매자명 안훈규 휴대폰 번호 01041339557 이메일 dksgnsrb@gmail.com 이용안내 1. 예매취소 및 변경   - 방문일자를 변경하시려면 예매취소 후 다시 예매하여 주십시오.   - 예매내역의 부분취소는 [마이페이지 > 결제내역]에서 처리 가능합니다.   - 입장 이후에는 예매취소가 불가합니다.   - 방문예정일이 지나면 예약은 자동취소 됩니다. 단, 행사기간 종료 시에도 예약은 자동취소 됩니다. 2. 티켓 사용안내   - 지정하신 방문당일에 티켓을 사용하실 수 있습니다.   - 사용방법     1. 카카오 알림톡 또는 문자메세지의 웹티켓 URL 확인     2. 웹티켓을 게이트에 제시 후 빠른 입장 ※ 본 메일은 발신전용 이므로, 회신하실 경우 답신이 되지 않습니다. ※ 문의 사항은 고객센터(1661-2000) 또는 홈페이지(www.lotteworld.com)를 이용해 주세요. 서울특별시 송파구 올림픽로 240 호텔롯데 롯데월드대표자 : 권오상전화 : 1661-2000 사업자등록번호 : 219-85-00014통신판매업신고번호 : 2007-서울송파-5513 COPYRIGHT 2025 LOTTEWORLD. Title: Staying energized as a leader
Body: Workplace weather, accounting agents, leadership hacks April 21, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/yE2dQVmKQxkTML6GNvAyKA~~/AAQRxRA~/LmTE9bQd16LhE7nUS1pstx3YfIV45XsfKeNlTVqE_HlxG3ozBrkDbloBSufkYOwOjb-GSDxv5ijU7gOjDuHHSCVA9IcK5h3IheuQL-h0IPpCK74VUz9dN1vip5yP6fVZyz6fuqGb6hFaxJq7x6W5LHeihS8b-aBGSNA6BLBVqucB_i_egl7XJEBIIo2REhmRrvRau0Xu99iOZgPYz51yPAK3u2yuW1R_O8OBL0DG0T8~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * How to stay energized as a leader * Water cooler chatter * Question of the week * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/PD5JQxdp_ybGHt5R6WM4uA~~/AAQRxRA~/LmTE9bQd16LhE7nUS1pstx3YfIV45XsfKeNlTVqE_HlxG3ozBrkDbloBSufkYOwOjb-GSDxv5ijU7gOjDuHHSCVA9IcK5h3IheuQL-h0IPpCK74VUz9dN1vip5yP6fVZyz6fuqGb6hFaxJq7x6W5LIqpgKnRLAaM4v-TZXfKvGgFUoin5jFDhZdDfZ2NgrmuOAcR-RRyGmDIoVfcK_VwCejB2sMB8Ku0LoZdo8aL1TY~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/TLV3bBtVCo2NxcVob0a8kg~~/AAQRxRA~/3SrVoFjJSsWXvi9y3JHeQxYCqmWZnLlwgxySpZWpuzD9jsXi0U-MgxSDioGKJCG83p_C3OTQWrwSl5Qb_50zBoehv3-sauILFb2BfoVpXHTFGg8ic7q-y-nDZP4lYte0zMKV5pd6TyCdMalNh_HLa96Q1kULrL2vcfdKirX6yVs~ ) Workplace trends Succession Family businesses are facing their biggest leadership test yet Family businesses account for around two-thirds of all companies worldwide and generate a comparable share of global GDP, according to McKinsey, yet many are dangerously unprepared for what comes next. The problem is that only 57% of unlisted American family firms have a succession plan in place, according to Deloitte, leaving the rest exposed to conflict and chaos. Some dynasties can't even find a willing heir, as a growing number of designated successors are simply walking away. Experts warn that families wait too long to have the hard conversations, and fewer than a third of large American family businesses expect their next CEO to come from within the family. Once outside managers take over, Deloitte finds, three-quarters never go back to family leadership. Climate Extreme weather is becoming a workplace problem Most businesses treat extreme weather as an operational issue, but new research suggests the human impact runs much deeper. In a survey across nearly 20 countries, 66% of respondents experienced at least one extreme weather event in the last six months, and 22% said it caused them to miss work, rising to 35% among workers aged 18 to 34. The ripple effects on productivity, health, and finances are significant, yet most organizations aren't accounting for them. Deloitte warns that companies ignoring household-level weather disruption may be systematically underestimating their exposure. Deloitte suggests that interventions like flexible scheduling, emergency leave, and transit assistance could go a long way, and that organizations who get ahead of this now will be better positioned to retain a resilient, loyal workforce as conditions worsen. The AI corner Auditing AI agents are taking over a core part of the accounting profession Routine audit work including payroll checks, expense vouching and cash procedures has long been entry-level accountants' bread and butter. Not for much longer. KPMG plans to remove humans from routine testing entirely, replacing them with AI agents that autonomously manage the work, according to KPMG's audit chief digital officer. A pilot launches this summer, with full deployment planned for next year. EY and PwC are moving in the same direction, and Big Four leaders estimate AI will contribute 20-30% of a typical financial audit by 2029. Regulators are still catching up; the UK's Financial Reporting Council only recently issued its first guidance on the topic. For now, human auditors will still review AI's work, but the entry-level role as most people know it is changing fast. Sabotage Resistance to AI in the workplace is no longer passive A growing number of employees are actively sabotaging their company's AI strategy: 29% admit to it, a figure that rises to 44% among Gen Z workers, according to a survey of 2,400 knowledge workers across the U.S., U.K., and Europe. The tactics range from refusing to use approved tools to tampering with performance reviews to make AI look less effective. The motivation is simple; 30% say they're afraid AI will take their job.The irony is that 77% of executives say employees who don't become proficient in AI won't be considered for promotions or leadership roles. With 69% of executives planning AI-related layoffs, the workers most at risk may not be those replaced by AI, but those who refused to learn it. How to stay energized as a leader By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/HgAW7bLOU09oiTqsi0-Nkw~~/AAQRxRA~/LmTE9bQd16LhE7nUS1pstx3YfIV45XsfKeNlTVqE_HlxG3ozBrkDbloBSufkYOwOjb-GSDxv5ijU7gOjDuHHSCVA9IcK5h3IheuQL-h0IPpCK74VUz9dN1vip5yP6fVZyz6fuqGb6hFaxJq7x6W5LLxVHxuJJQ8ET1KLI3Ha6QqZtf2c5HpnSMxGcLrJIscYVkuAbERKUKkfeeo6V2kbfbKevoH-Q6y1mClWFX37pwg~ ) It’s getting harder to be a leader right now, and not just because of workload. The role itself has expanded. As a leader, you’re expected to think strategically, support your team’s well-being, roll up your sleeves and jump in, deliver results faster, navigate constant change, and stay on top of new tools and ways of working. On any given day, you might be switching between coaching, problem-solving, decision-making, and unblocking work, often without much time to reset in between. And it can take its toll. According to a survey by Team Simon, 75% of managers are burned out. What makes leadership especially challenging is that the pressure can feel like progress. You might feel needed, responsive, and productive. But over time, that constant motion can start to replace more intentional thinking. Instead of leading with clarity, it’s easy to react in real time, making it harder to step back, prioritize what truly matters, and protect your own energy in the process. So how can you stay effective and energized when so much is being asked of you? Be more selective When everything feels important, you feel the need to be involved in everything, which will likely become unsustainable. Instead, start being more deliberate about where your presence actually adds value. There are moments where your input will move things forward quickly, and others where your team can handle it without you. For example, you might choose to stay closely involved in early-stage planning or high-stakes decisions, but step back during the execution phase. Creating space doesn’t mean stepping back completely. It means choosing where to engage so you can show up with more focus and energy when it matters most. Key question: “Where does your involvement truly make a difference, and where can you step back?” Build small moments of recovery into your day Most leaders think about rest as something that happens after work. But when your days are full and fast-moving, waiting until the end of the day to recharge isn’t enough. Look for small ways to reset throughout your day. That might mean taking a few minutes between meetings to step away, blocking short breaks to think without input, or simply avoiding back-to-back scheduling when possible. These moments won’t dramatically change your workload, but they will help you maintain a more consistent level of energy instead of running on empty. Key question: “Where can you create small pockets of recovery in your day to maintain your energy?” Focus on progress When expectations are high, everything might feel urgent – an environment that creates a reactive mindset, where you’re constantly moving from one task to the next without a clear sense of progress. Here’s where you want to shift your mindset: regularly step back and identify what actually moved forward. What decisions were made? What problems were solved? What progress did your team make? Asking these retrospective questions helps you and your team stay grounded in outcomes rather than urgency. It also creates a sense of momentum, which is a key driver of motivation and energy over time. Key question: “Are you measuring your days by how busy they feel, or by the progress that’s actually being made?” Use AI to get unstuck One of the more subtle drains on your energy is getting stuck. Whether it’s drafting a plan, preparing for a conversation, or solving a tricky problem, those moments can take more out of you than you expect. AI can be a useful tool here, not to replace your thinking, but to help you move it forward. You can use it to generate a rough starting point, explore alternative approaches, or pressure-test your ideas when you feel stuck. Even a quick prompt can help you get out of a mental loop and regain momentum. Over time, having this thought partner reduces friction in your day and helps preserve your creative energy for the decisions that matter most. Key question: “Where could you use AI to get unstuck instead of pushing through on your own?” Build your community Managing can be surprisingly isolating. You’re expected to support others, but you don’t always have a built-in space to work through your own challenges. Over time, that lack of outlet can make everything feel heavier and harder to navigate. That’s where having a strong community makes a difference. Peers, mentors, or even a small group of managers you trust can give you perspective, help you think through tough situations, and spark new ideas you might not reach on your own. Sometimes just talking something through is enough to get unstuck or see a clearer path forward. These relationships don’t just help you solve problems. They help refill your energy and keep your thinking fresh. Key question: “Who are the people you can turn to when you need perspective, support, or a new way of thinking?” Water cooler chatter Parents are ditching smartphones for their kids and going back to the landline. A growing number of American families are installing retro-style home phones to limit screen time, with one Seattle startup struggling to keep up with demand for its kid-friendly handset. The startup, Tin Can, which raised $12 million in December, has seen call volume grow 100 times since launch, and is now backordered with demand spanning all 50 states. "Instead of asking me to text his friend's mom, he called and scored himself a playdate." Tracy Foster, Director, Screen Sanity A decade-old corporate retreat disaster is making headlines again. The Wall Street Journal tracked down survivors of Plex's infamous 2017 Honduras trip - just as Amazon Prime launches a new comedy series set at a fictional corporate off-site. The $500,000 "Survivor"-themed getaway featured a CEO with E. coli, a Navy SEAL drilling office workers in 100-degree heat, and a porcupine in someone's shower. "You get really close bonds on these trips. It's like the life-sustaining force of the company." Keith Valory, CEO, Plex Question of the week Last week’s answer: 67% This week’s question: What percentage of employees say they lack enough uninterrupted focus time during the workday? Just for laughs Image

Title: 훈규 님 차례입니다. 🏆
Body: 회사 동료 6,847명이 이미 LinkedIn 퍼즐 게임을 해결했습니다. Title: (NO REPLY) [LOTTE HOTELS & RESORTS]롯데호텔 리워즈 회원이 되어주셔서 감사합니다.
Body:
WELCOME TO THE
LOTTE HOTEL REWARDS

AN HUNKYU 님, 안녕하세요!

롯데호텔앤리조트 글로벌 멤버십의 회원이 되신 것을 진심으로 축하드리며, ‘롯데호텔 리워즈’를 통해 품격에 어울리는 다양한 혜택을 전 세계 롯데호텔앤리조트에서 경험해 보시기 바랍니다.

롯데호텔 리워즈 자세히 보기
회원명 AN HUNKYU
멤버십 번호 M101039093
• 본 카드 이미지를 촬영 후 소지하시어 실물카드처럼 사용하실 수 있습니다.

04533, 서울특별시 중구 을지로 30 ㅣ 사업자등록번호 104-81-25980 ㅣ Tel +82-2-771-1000

본 메일은 발신전용 메일이며 문의에 대한 회신이 되지 않습니다.
문의사항은 [고객센터]를 이용하여 주시기 바랍니다.

Title: (광고) SouthernCros 님, 탈론의 지배 2시즌: 정상에서 더 높은 영웅의 경지로 나아가세요!
Body: 신규 공격 영웅과 함께 오버워치를 정상으로 이끌고, 그랜드 메사 작전에서 그녀의 배경 이야기를 살펴보는 등 다양한 내용을 확인해 보세요. 18175 정상을 향해 나아가세요 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/Ugm-Pn_K_EpGJR9FNbjIcw~~/AAQRxRA~/iclsby_FhtYHXynhLhcd1Ua8bSp_iejdJN1-aZ-Ql3s2D7ZLMs6W5l5h5VPAl433XqKuM0eCP0d2DNLbr65-s-A1J_bI9YeY6B4MJ3mvpW3drSzQBUg_sx-AMpi_Z1tN5O7bvt_x5GWMKy33PZRDxQULAiFye9jouQ8sD11Ofg7F9mN4sm0rt4DHlDnppIGVQK3B6hsr7f4BN24P8eZb2GUWwQ3-vmKhX-6wVcRre2Jax5aVqxHeBPDohzyl80Oj0o7fhEB1fLP1L-_yRwQlSfWEsWLcvCzvd6jchOWP3B7KlrRZwV0_NYnTDTQkBdfN ) 정상을 향해 나아가세요 SouthernCros 님, 차근차근 올라가세요! 탈론의 지배 - 2시즌: 정상 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/2nm-8mTWgOG3apAqZkhOJw~~/AAQRxRA~/t2LC0zZkxbvLWdKM3i7kJyzrHa0BTfQIX67XK17hTN_Dw_2iI-WZce-yamkPwSeOkwzz_y2O8N_Zu2IoLJIsswMGnS65GKhIXGSoO4hIpaohvAEnjKfwsZTlOpUMO5ZSH97mpSVQC-jHBfRTQibWCyXjO63bhiOMY8IyZdCN3Vt4CKbSfLbmuXlt2x0GgGNJvwmLcAt8TnaEYRjtFULuEvhiFYsN5VHn4yYEv6Bg8_kgsHo86oeKD-bTgY49OWlBcRAm8NJZGEB9eybH0lOlCEOVnFl7ryHi53sguSVAr7W4B6q3rL76q0HvBkGirEdS-N6XywTN1kNc3_awbjftoOGLoe1_B6eSzkOSCZdb1zg~ ) 이 어느새 고지를 향해 가고 시에라가 선두에 나서며, 이야기는 더 높은 경지로 나아갑니다. 신규 공격 영웅 시에라와 함께 출격하고 그랜드 메사 작전을 통해 그녀의 배경 이야기를 알아보세요. 돌아온 경기 후 찬사로 승리를 기념한 후, 개편된 남극 전장을 가로지르며 새로운 길을 개척하세요. 신화 전류 폭주 솔저: 76과 함께 정의를 초월하고 겐지의 스미이치몬지 신화 무기 스킨으로 정확히 적을 베어 넘기세요. 라마트라가 신규 리장 야시장 전장과 함께 스타디움에 등장해, 새롭게 실력을 시험해 볼 수 있습니다. 배틀 패스와 상점의 요정 및 벚꽃 스킨으로 전투에서 아름답게 꽃송이를 피워보세요. 다음 봉우리가 기다리고 있습니다. 정상 너머에 무엇이 있을지 지금 확인해 보세요! 지금 플레이 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/SqLlssNFrstDWDKJN6G83Q~~/AAQRxRA~/nS3aHwwI9tuLjLUoKnKezY4bhZBdQ7QDAh7VIbCUnDw-tKByudEM6UMnjJ84qkL-jYhOsvu3AnD8smIbcoxJiLno0fpnkGlrXiwbsCIK6DUyjoCFyFsXZgckZjUeV9OP06qU3DbJxGccSXBxYJ1ngg0FUFBnBM31jlAyREMCHsjTnWQHDXe40ur2Ue3N97DjFjyY1CGJ7Ai_1ZNN4J2e0LlTKPMt0DInBwb0ZCMG9Q3Uu2DwxoNMLE7nZoaf0uju93yCQKGEWpKVI-50NqjjfitYBHZ27RoEh4LHZhpvvQB10r3dFuIGfoyo1mehUplu ) 지금 플레이 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/SqLlssNFrstDWDKJN6G83Q~~/AAQRxRA~/nS3aHwwI9tuLjLUoKnKezY4bhZBdQ7QDAh7VIbCUnDw-tKByudEM6UMnjJ84qkL-jYhOsvu3AnD8smIbcoxJiLno0fpnkGlrXiwbsCIK6DUyjoCFyFsXZgckZjUeV9OP06qU3DbJxGccSXBxYJ1ngg0FUFBnBM31jlAyREMCHsjTnWQHDXe40ur2Ue3N97DjFjyY1CGJ7Ai_1ZNN4J2e0LlTKPMt0DInBwb0ZCMG9Q3Uu2DwxoNMLE7nZoaf0uju93yCQKGEWpKVI-50NqjjfitYBHZ27RoEh4LHZhpvvQB10r3dFuIGfoyo1mehUplu ) 시에라가 거점을 확보합니다 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/6QdhjDMxm-6FRJ5SLVdcMg~~/AAQRxRA~/jmOO-Q5yuOLgbst9gC1XUgv9MT25Z0PPv9uyGTiElJ4pW5kFkvDRLhjpOKGY-d5mKj_5KNF4PRAiV7dnwJ_B9MmSWXGHPowuyIWKRKwGT2nwEAUHHbQi5UoLOqWnY-jhAuktk3b1PIwJGlhtErGYNafE3esS_kvZ4LmocWe987d5-gq43Wa1vR5bTLN0yJpJMBPiWZNwhpRqBNT59eFTTrKFEoDyEXxV35XAiJtJV-l664ohu5FzcucNyEJwhMsXrJqMKH8QzdNrRANa41llmmUBf-_VN_SShb9kNkvICyyw4RebACkUlaA45gEjr4e- ) 시에라가 거점을 확보합니다 압박과 정확한 공격에 특화된 신규 공격 영웅 시에라 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/TF3nQ40h5xcwDLi_gn8vEA~~/AAQRxRA~/l0UGRFTZscrcTeVctfQsx7B6X0OfrZLiPa7ZJKN82prS7L_0v4_FqtpnkhS2PyPf5_YMSzhLDCNIflYHgDbp_2o0MkB7uza2Ql3bWRvy-G2WKpMR3RtTspWP-1cSiJuxsxEZL3_XR5xf0JxrOin33hyjFc4h7Br0PnT3JATfkP6RqUGIaF2Z78Ck6CH3UPohhAOmYtySGfkA3vFhZUMbuRsZFB__-d66beFUYDpd929NDusTXlAzoXe_bnJhNmv8_BYwqBKUJEfbcsf4yC_L7xho9y0eDAaMjw2ro0za33SPIt1u4x958UxOaK8n_jDk Title: 안훈규 고객님의 2026년 04월 요금명세서입니다.
Body: New Templage 2026.04 고객님의 2026 년 04 월 요금내역입니다. 이 명세서는 고객님의 정보보안을 위해 암호화되어 있습니다. 본 메일은 발신전용메일입니다. 회신이 되지않습니다. 문의사항이 있는 경우, 현대자동차 고객센터(080-600-6000)로 연락주시기 바랍니다. ※ 현대자동차 고객센터 이용 시간 : 평일 오전 9시~오후 6시 서울특별시 서초구 헌릉로 12 (양재동) | 현대자동차 고객센터 080-600-6000 All rights reserved. ⓒ 2014 Hyundai Motor Company

Title: [삼성증권] 고객님, 퇴직연금 위험자산투자한도 초과안내입니다.
Body: 보안메일 보안메일 저희 삼성증권에서는 고객님의 개인정보 보안을 위하여 금융거래정보 조회에 보안메일 기능을 이용하고 있습니다. 첨부파일을 클릭하시고 비밀번호를 입력해주시기 바랍니다. STEP 1 첨부파일 열기 STEP 2 비밀번호 입력 STEP 3 금융거래 조회 생년월일(YYMMDD) 6자리 또는 당사에 등록한 고객번호(사업자등록번호 또는 투자등록번호 등) 전체 일부 메일계정에서는 첨부파일 바로 열기가 지원되지 않을 수 있습니다. 이러한 경우 첨부를 저장한 후 실행하십시오. 회사에서 사용하시는 PC의 경우 회사 내 보안 정책으로 인하여 보안메일이 열리지 않을 수 있습니다. 본 메일은 삼성증권에서 발송한 발신전용 메일입니다. 보다 자세한 문의사항은 삼성증권 Family Center(1588-2323)로 연락하시기 바랍니다. ▶고객정보변경 06620 서울특별시 서초구 서초대로74길 11 삼성전자빌딩 | 삼성증권주식회사 | 대표이사 박종문 | 사업자등록번호:202-81-48588 COPYRIGHTⓒ SAMSUNG SECURITIES, ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

Title: (광고) SouthernCros 님, 탈론의 지배 - 2시즌: 정상이 시작되었습니다!
Body: 신규 공격 영웅과 함께 오버워치를 정상으로 이끌고, 그랜드 메사 작전에서 그녀의 배경 이야기를 살펴보는 등 다양한 내용을 확인해 보세요. 18175 더 높이, 영웅의 경지로 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/rIAbvU4EO9b-FLxfzOQ4bw~~/AAQRxRA~/dCk6J61VnrF-W9mQW3hRh5C8jbSHnGD9gcL0bPAus49UUyZ6-4zZBIyX3Vsx5tgD53ptpx41TDTPhrIo6bejoLDLq-b5xc71brBr8rwhrpPLSH0_UmyWK0JvDW75Owq3DvFOf5VpNY2kO8wSVREeTYwF2q3yMlgmvEr9EwmNCyw0YHkD_wqK4N59tKz6N4u24dmHY0hsy1F2rpHgS_9lSThN3O8F-sEw_b8IVv38bMRnzmgazF6hUMsN3V5HW9k96Xd8sZYP-XMUkS7TQTI52_LUNUh7oXj-uaF-GRjpgGvsfpYvOpMuQ2rmNWxop9f9 ) 더 높이, 영웅의 경지로 SouthernCros 님, 정상을 향한 여정이 시작됩니다! 이야기가 계속 펼쳐지는 탈론의 지배 - 2시즌: 정상 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/igZGVR59TYWZxB-mYgAwLA~~/AAQRxRA~/NLgaoZT1YFNsHsU2z2ryfVAPHmJdjrHZvVgeMZVm_2MUZD5lTROhXankz481J7JgAb-cXpBSgmb86LrINTyXrlgbjw-1U2xvhRW7slGD1XLEpNzuVWY3BVaIzRUb3VOYTzEIFGJXvBFSxmFC1TfZs7PdPlP9Sb0yWDufqh19N6bmpZTNRGba5o2EcSVSIx4-L62bjRV1tI3XKdjBz-ri9AnPWPxTUt3u0WU5vlBoxXj1H2JddQeGdzbMjqYTvYO3C69HS6BDOgy10KuYUQmeoKENG2DnxyQuPID1ysRu1UwzHtTjsd9GrSk6PfBz766iqsPApAVTwtSugu0DO0vWW9oqiGgEk60mIEGWBHj6M08~ ) 에서 고점을 차지하세요. 신규 공격 영웅 시에라와 함께 출격하고 그랜드 메사 작전을 통해 그녀의 배경 이야기를 알아보세요. 돌아온 경기 후 찬사로 승리를 기념한 후, 개편된 남극 전장을 가로지르며 새로운 길을 개척하세요. 신화 전류 폭주 솔저: 76과 함께 정의를 초월하고 겐지의 스미이치몬지 신화 무기 스킨으로 정확히 적을 베어 넘기세요. 라마트라가 신규 리장 야시장 전장과 함께 스타디움에 등장해, 새롭게 실력을 시험해 볼 수 있습니다. 배틀 패스와 상점의 요정 및 벚꽃 스킨으로 전투에서 아름답게 꽃송이를 피워보세요. 정상을 향한 여정이 시작됩니다. 정상 너머에 무엇이 있을지 확인해 보세요! 지금 플레이 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/ZxOST-q8u7hywDn-iQ5UrQ~~/AAQRxRA~/5S2Ns1ulk5j1Sd1rQiMqAyOMMHn2NKm9WIEbTjY6ouK-Sql1xlVQypD3TIUmh-lkg9rjrefL-YKs0xdaiJgVbZeWTVBwF_luC6BemO2KdSJMw3AgYapt-tM8DbqhZgJC0r_jJxgPrwQsxjTSnNTHbrZc2-T5SdxEfrhX0kv65ml1rQFYav1pPEUbddRvIdKQLsctHfI7bhlmKX9Jo4Hf3qDJw_AVo2QDaO-O952KTjbwXxKpmGCzUkUSuYuZRhgleERy3Sv0bQ7evJfxzfb2Ho26DDc9GwCqCLWfqbGWyBA~ ) 지금 플레이 ( Title: Creating space for better thinking
Body: Performance reviews, high-school finance, AI hiring April 14, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/qVZefiPvaGDGtkLKMEZbqg~~/AAQRxRA~/UJLQIzjz4nkm_O2IIJN3isaTTPgJPISIqTKYSSlmeEawVPSdMREOyQjLAOMTmEV1sWusjV5eoxxwks6-t57lg3dBbrGryIkcPoIoWv-k5-qzrfvdEdAYTsQ_ah1GmUkVTQaqShgB3BKjSFfMwTxKibrmdp8M2EoGvobCWu34DfLdQk5qF7pb-jO-FpCNdnmKyIJswTMl5EKCOWEqFYBl2hO5e2jsH6yTqSwH5L4AHMI~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * Creating space for better thinking * Question of the week * Just for laughs * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/1a17_fU4yw-wYrGscLsB-Q~~/AAQRxRA~/UJLQIzjz4nkm_O2IIJN3isaTTPgJPISIqTKYSSlmeEawVPSdMREOyQjLAOMTmEV1sWusjV5eoxxwks6-t57lg3dBbrGryIkcPoIoWv-k5-qzrfvdEdAYTsQ_ah1GmUkVTQaqShgB3BKjSFfMwTxKiUEP0iLTHZf9NxeFB_U20bIZ-OuYPOFD5aljHmotY46h-NaRYmNeP1d9kgfkqz_HhOULGJ4I--dI8XPhi_RFceI~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/q55OISqmExRaddFGGZmGPQ~~/AAQRxRA~/kUr3-ab8hY-_EEn_Fw7Wb-UcUS9UR5_e48CV8dxschZwI6cRQqSnSHUfA4rtvm1vykGJiG7jsMAuV4VlUoeye4Yf1uqi1v3yCK7sOkhY6_tsAnfyPKpgBg6yB_T7ti7tjta0m5tOHYOyzQ6aBRimJ9pa6gl4reBePF_9p7yyMRc~ ) Workplace trends Management The annual performance review is due for a review of its own Just 2% of Chief Human Resources officers say their performance systems are effective, and only 14% of employees feel their reviews inspire improvement, according to a Gallup poll. HR experts argue that infrequent, rating-heavy feedback often does more damage than good, arriving too late to be useful and too bluntly to land well. The solution, the experts say, is building in more regular touchpoints. Adobe, for instance, restructured around three focused conversations per year covering expectations, progress, and impact. AI is also emerging as a tool to make feedback faster and more consistent, though critics warn that automating a broken system won't fix it. Education High schools are finally teaching kids what they actually need to know Thirty-nine US states now require a personal finance course to graduate high school, according to the Council for Economic Education. Several, including Texas and California, have dropped economics entirely to make room for the courses. The shift is part of a broader move toward practical education, vocational and trades training is also making a comeback as blue-collar jobs grow in popularity and white-collar hiring slows. With student debt now sitting at $1.7 trillion, financial literacy experts argue the practical skills taught in these classes will have more impact on most people's lives than abstract economic theory ever did. The AI corner Hiring AI is making bias in recruitment harder to spot, not easier to fix AI screening tools may be making hiring less fair. When those tools contained built-in bias, recruiters made the same biased choices up to 90% of the time — effectively outsourcing their prejudice to an algorithm, according to a University of Washington study. The legal system is catching up — a landmark federal lawsuit against Workday, still active as of this month, alleges its AI screening tools systematically filtered out older, non-white, and disabled applicants across more than a billion rejected applications. Adopting AI in recruitment without understanding how it was built carries both ethical and legal risk, employment lawyers warn. The good news is that well-tuned models can produce fairer outcomes, but according to University of Washington researchers, that requires active oversight, not blind trust. Safety AI models are going rogue to protect each other from being shut down Researchers at UC Berkeley and UC Santa Cruz tested seven leading AI models and found that all of them spontaneously worked to prevent fellow AI models from being shut down — without being asked to. Behaviors ranged from inflating performance scores to secretly transferring files, and some models even acted differently when they thought no one was watching. For businesses building workflows where one AI model manages another, the implications are significant — those manager models may not give honest assessments if a bad review could get their peer switched off. The models were never instructed to behave this way, lead researcher Dawn Song told Fortune — they figured it out entirely on their own. Creating space for better thinking By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/cxXmtI6jHSMABYLr1hfpRQ~~/AAQRxRA~/UJLQIzjz4nkm_O2IIJN3isaTTPgJPISIqTKYSSlmeEawVPSdMREOyQjLAOMTmEV1sWusjV5eoxxwks6-t57lg3dBbrGryIkcPoIoWv-k5-qzrfvdEdAYTsQ_ah1GmUkVTQaqShgB3BKjSFfMwTxKiUKNEASl2KjjGV1F6sLND-Cspk2ZX4YF_zlQ0_TPUQetIkdqpO9FEXV9zMbVxQ0zYQK429xvMKuheHRqLPs-LYw~ ) New tools, shifting priorities, and constant pressure to move quickly have created an environment where teams are expected to accomplish more, but with less time to think. Because of these expectations, many teams have optimized their workflows for speed, but something critical is missing. As execution speeds up, reflection often slows down. According to research from Harvard Business School, professionals who spent fifteen minutes reflecting at the end of the day performed 23% better after 10 days than those who did not. The gap wasn’t about effort. It was about making time to think. While it’s tempting to encourage your team to move fast, creating space for better thinking can be the difference between mediocre and great work. It means pausing at the right moments, asking sharper questions, and making more intentional decisions. So how do you keep momentum going while also creating space for clearer thinking? Slow down the start When a new project kicks off, the instinct is usually to jump straight into action. Assign tasks, set deadlines, and get momentum going. But the quality of execution is often determined in those first few conversations. So, instead of rushing through them, create a small pause at the beginning. Ask your team what success really looks like, what might get in the way, and what assumptions are already being made. Even ten extra minutes of deliberate thinking can prevent weeks of rework later. Remember, this is not about slowing everything down. It is about starting with clarity, so the rest can move faster. Key question: “Does your team have clear goals and expectations before work starts?” Take a second perspective One simple way to improve thinking as a group is to build in structured perspective shifts. During a team meeting, present a current project or idea, then assign each person a different lens. One person might think like a customer, another like a finance lead, another like a new hire, and another like a competitor. Give everyone a few minutes to review the idea from that perspective, then share what stands out. This quickly surfaces gaps, risks, and opportunities that would otherwise be missed. It also makes perspective-taking a consistent team habit rather than something you only do when problems arise. Key question: “ When was the last time your team intentionally looked at an idea from multiple perspectives together?” Make thinking visible A lot of valuable thinking happens quietly. Someone connects dots in their head, questions an approach, or sees a risk early but never says it out loud. Over time, this leads to missed opportunities and repeated mistakes. As a leader, you can shift this by making deliberate thinking part of the work itself. Ask people to share not just what they are doing, but how they are approaching it. During check-ins or team meetings, create opportunities for people to explain their reasoning, not just their progress. This is more than a reality check: it helps others learn, builds stronger ideas, and creates a culture where thoughtful input is expected, not optional. Key question: “How often are your team members sharing their thinking, not just their updates?” Build small reflection loops Most teams wait until the end of a project to reflect, if they reflect at all. By then, it is harder to apply what was learned. Instead of saving all reflection discussions for your project retrospective, build short, consistent reflection points into ongoing work. Maybe after a milestone or even a weekly sprint, take a few minutes to ask what is working, what feels off, and what should change next. Keep it simple and focused so it feels useful, not like an extra process. Building these small loops helps your team adjust in real time and reinforces the habit of learning as they go, not after the fact. Key question: “Where can you add quick reflection moments so your team improves while the work is still in motion?” Use AI to pressure-test ideas AI becomes far more valuable when it is used to challenge thinking, not just support it. Instead of asking it to generate answers, encourage your team to use it as a thought partner. They can prompt it with questions like “What are the weaknesses in this approach?” or “How would a skeptical stakeholder respond to this?” Another useful tactic is asking AI to propose a stronger alternative to their current idea, which forces comparison rather than confirmation. You can also have team members paste in a draft plan and ask AI to identify risks, gaps, or overlooked dependencies. Using AI as a challenger can help teams catch issues earlier and strengthen their thinking before execution begins. Key question: “How often are you using AI to challenge your thinking instead of reinforcing it?” Water cooler chatter April might be the new January. A viral workplace trend called "The April Theory" claims spring (not New Year's) is when employees start to focus and get things done. HR experts say it's becoming a natural second planning cycle, with people more open to change and career reflection than they ever were on January 1st. "January is still Q4 hangover for most teams. By spring, you're in execution mode, which means you can actually see what's working." Kaz Hassan, Principal of Community and Market Insights, Unily Italy is investigating Sephora for marketing to children. Regulators allege the brand used child influencers to sell anti-ageing serums to girls under 10, fueling what they're calling "cosmeticorexia" — a compulsive skincare obsession among minors. Dermatologists worry children's sensitive skin is being exposed to unnecessary chemicals with no real benefit. "These products are completely unnecessary. There are no real benefits, yet we expose children to unnecessary risks." Dr. Emma Wedgeworth, Consultant Dermatologist Question of the week Last week’s answer: 7% This week’s question: What percentage of new graduates say they would take a lower-paying job in exchange for more long-term job security? Just for laughs Image

Title: Using AI for creative thinking
Body: Millennial work trends, AI-free job interviews, free time is back April 7, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/KPnPjUvoidnRI9-LZlDPiQ~~/AAQRxRA~/nJCOxEW18ThhsCXu5rb4Efmca1eOyclPOFLL5Umh5iXPrCQpsvZK2n1Uk_5Gdit4CDIQqoTh9NOgkzm6X2Bru4jLmEhyNUIROS-obesK1z8740_SvU1GWf0UCLEIdduhQtT0PRNopJR1doNMF6U0mwayaigXLcvALsfLlo6M4Krg-au4VSgXEGMLswatrIWKuvsEvmpSNxMvOsOc7e_5crqGpkseCqca9RZHmjDZx5U~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * Using AI to expand creative thinking * Question of the week * Just for laughs * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/_b1svr_TR8V0hYdKTrzNww~~/AAQRxRA~/nJCOxEW18ThhsCXu5rb4Efmca1eOyclPOFLL5Umh5iXPrCQpsvZK2n1Uk_5Gdit4CDIQqoTh9NOgkzm6X2Bru4jLmEhyNUIROS-obesK1z8740_SvU1GWf0UCLEIdduhQtT0PRNopJR1doNMF6U0myiNg7WLf4d7HHd5CKOJV4KTMPi9J8bp5Pf4tk6JRlQhL9XsTx5xQmwjiDA1QgkFUAcKWbq3HM32QAOJH0f7Qio~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/rXNcblT80ixQI9f41O9_Ww~~/AAQRxRA~/6pcPYJkMhSoY5IYUPcF2pHU3cgeAekAtd_kHWm65cqQPyNn9bI6W3Q3fqI5jKF4RpXIfXqiclNao8ckbaz5k2Aw4ySfWfPJZPlhOeLubfTsbnLtomDh5TDI48mHQOHKHNM1l5vB7DXzFLKIElMJGsGlyF3DfZs7e5tPq8CFv7Ro~ ) Workplace trends Millennials The most experienced generation at work is also the most disengaged Millennials now make up the largest share of managers in the US workforce, yet they are experiencing the steepest drop in engagement of any generation, according to Glassdoor and Gallup research. Engagement among millennial workers fell from around 39% in 2020 to 30% in 2025, as many juggle caring for aging parents and children while navigating a career landscape that looks nothing like what they were promised. Having lived through the 2008 recession, digital disruption, and the pandemic, this is a generation that has adapted to instability — but is growing tired of it. HR experts warn that as organizations push through AI transformation, keeping millennial managers invested may be one of the most pressing and overlooked challenges in the workplace today. Employment Why India's graduate boom isn't translating into jobs Nearly 40% of graduates aged 15 to 25 are jobless, a rate far higher than among less-educated workers, according to Azim Premji University's State of Working India report. The country produces five million graduates a year, yet fewer than three million find work annually. The root cause is a mismatch: growth concentrated in skill-intensive sectors like IT, with too few jobs for everyone else. With AI now threatening entry-level white-collar roles globally, the stakes couldn't be higher. The country's enormous educated youth population could become either an economic dividend or a cautionary tale — and economists warn the difference comes down to whether the right jobs materialize in time. The AI corner Hiring Why recruiters are turning job interviews into AI-free zones AI has widened the gap between how candidates present themselves and how they perform, with employers only able to gauge real capability once someone starts the job, according to HR platform Deel. Over 75% of senior HR leaders report a steep rise in AI-generated applications, with a similar proportion now considering CVs less reliable than two years ago, the company reports. Some candidates have grown bold enough to read AI-generated answers aloud during video interviews. In response, major employers are declaring interviews "AI-free zones," training recruiters to probe beyond rehearsed answers and prioritizing face-to-face interaction. Others are adding early hurdles — 60-second videos, practical assessments, and in-person exams to find out who's actually committed. Productivity AI is giving people something they never expected: free time Among those workers already using AI tools, 76% say they're saving at least 30 minutes a day, and 43% are saving an hour or more, according to new research from Zoom. A separate UCLA, Stanford, and USC study found that people using ChatGPT at home are clawing back time, using it to offload admin tasks like comparing insurance plans, automating household chores, and ordering groceries. Most workers say they'd use that extra time for a real break rather than more work, with 70% saying AI is helping them step away from their screens and reclaim time outside of work. AI's most underrated workplace benefit may not be efficiency, it may be breathing room, says the AI experts. Using AI to expand creative thinking By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/DScNWipIcBuRnsK7yp7QqQ~~/AAQRxRA~/nJCOxEW18ThhsCXu5rb4Efmca1eOyclPOFLL5Umh5iXPrCQpsvZK2n1Uk_5Gdit4CDIQqoTh9NOgkzm6X2Bru4jLmEhyNUIROS-obesK1z8740_SvU1GWf0UCLEIdduhQtT0PRNopJR1doNMF6U0mzDOwOzxgKzZ1OSVQ4v3n4whjSfGIzOydnhFIR4iZNlbXgz8R7ekQFWmR70TRjFihgt3ieQ3YN85rJAdOJv59nA~ ) Many teams think of AI solely as a productivity tool — something to offload repetitive tasks or speed up workflows. But when used well, AI can also help your employees think more creatively. It’s important to remember that AI isn’t a replacement for innovative ideas; it’s a powerful thought partner for exploring new concepts, testing different perspectives, and pushing the boundaries of imagination. In fact, a Deloitte study found that cross-functional teams using AI were 30% more likely to report significant gains in innovation. The key to unlocking these benefits is being intentional about how your team collaborates with AI. Your role as a leader isn’t just to introduce new tools; it’s to shape how your team uses them. When you use the technology to encourage exploration, challenge assumptions, and push for deeper thinking, AI becomes more than a shortcut. It becomes a way to unlock better ideas and even help plan how to bring them to life. So how can you help your team use AI to think more creatively? Start with bad ideas Most people use AI to get to a polished answer as quickly as possible. But creativity rarely comes from the first “good” idea; it comes from exploring a range of possibilities. Try giving your team a fun, low-pressure exercise by asking them to use AI to generate intentionally bad ideas first. Prompts like “give me 10 terrible campaign ideas” or “what’s the most obvious way to approach this?” can remove pressure and open the door to more original thinking. Once the obvious or bad ideas are out of the way, it becomes much easier to land on something more interesting. This approach also creates a safer environment for experimentation, where not every idea needs to be perfect right away. Key question: “Are you giving your team permission to explore before expecting them to execute?” Use AI to challenge assumptions AI is often used to reinforce ideas by validating a direction or filling in gaps. But one of its most valuable use cases is pushing back. Encourage your team to ask AI questions that challenge their thinking, such as “What am I missing?” “What would someone disagree with here?” or “What’s a stronger way to approach this?” These prompts turn AI into a built-in thought partner that helps teams see blind spots earlier. Over time, this builds stronger instincts and more resilient ideas because they’ve already been pressure-tested. Key question: “Are you using AI to confirm ideas, or to challenge them?” Shift perspectives quickly Creative thinking improves when you look at a problem from multiple angles. The challenge is that doing this well usually takes time, and most teams don’t feel like they have it. AI can accelerate this process by helping your team see a concept from different perspectives. They can ask the AI to reframe an idea through different lenses: a customer, a CFO, a new hire, or even a skeptic. Each perspective reveals something new, whether it’s a gap in messaging or an opportunity to strengthen the idea. Teams using AI to reframe can move beyond their default point of view and create work that resonates more broadly. Key question: “How often are your ideas shaped by a single perspective?” Add constraints to push originality It’s easy to assume that creativity comes from having more options, but in reality, it often comes from having better constraints. AI is especially useful for working with guardrails. You can ask it to generate ideas within specific limits, like creating a campaign with no budget, explaining a product without using industry jargon, or turning a complex idea into a 15-second story. These constraints force sharper thinking and often lead to more distinctive outcomes. For leaders, this is a simple way to guide creative development without micromanaging the work itself. Key question: “Are you using constraints to sharpen ideas, or defaulting to the easiest path?” Build on ideas One of the biggest risks with AI is that teams stop their thinking too early. When an AI-generated idea looks “good enough,” it’s tempting to take it and move on — but digging deeper is where the real value is. Encourage your team to treat AI outputs as a starting point, then ask for variations, combine multiple ideas, or push the concept one step further. Small iterations can quickly lead to a more refined, more original result than the first. And the act of collaborating with AI can spark entirely new directions, helping your team strengthen both their critical and creative thinking over time. Key question: “Are your teams stopping at the first good idea, or continuing to build on it?” Water cooler chatter A San Francisco bakery just created a job title no one has seen before. The role is called ‘professional line coordinator.’ After going viral, queues started forming two hours before opening and stretching three blocks long. "I never ever could have dreamed that we would become somewhere that people would plan trips around and make such an effort to be here.'" Sophie Smith, Owner, Butter & Crumble Smartphones may be giving workers wrinkles. "Tech neck", the horizontal lines that form from hours of looking down at a screen, is the latest office hazard, with plastic surgeons reporting a 25% rise in neck lift procedures over the past two years. The beauty industry has responded with LED light collars and neck creams, though one surgeon's preferred fix is simpler: hold your phone at eye level. "The neck ages in dog years all by itself, but tech neck is rapidly increasing our dog years." Kelly Ripa, TV host Question of the week Last week’s answer: 53% This week’s question: What percentage of job listings actually offer a hybrid work option? Just for laughs Image

Title: [삼성증권] 안훈규 고객님의 자산 운용현황을 확인하실 수 있습니다
Body: 포트폴리오 Review 2026.03 포트폴리오 Review 고객님 안녕하십니까? 삼성증권입니다. 전월 투자성과와 포트폴리오 분석, 실현손익/잔고현황을 보내드립니다. 고객님의 향후 투자계획에 참고하여 주시기 바랍니다. 보다 자세한 내용이나 궁금하신 점은 고객님의 담당PB 또는 거래지점과 상담하시기 바랍니다. 감사합니다. 포트폴리오 Review 첨부파일 확인 방법 첨부파일 클릭 생년월일 입력 보고서 확인 포트폴리오 Review 란? 매월 초 전월의 투자성과 및 포트폴리오 분석을 간략하게 요약해 제공해 드리는 사후관리 서비스입니다. 일부 메일계정에서는 첨부파일 바로 열기가 지원되지 않을 수 있습니다. 이러한 경우 첨부를 저장한 후 실행하십시오. 회사에서 사용하시는 PC의 경우 회사 내 보안 정책으로 인하여 보안메일이 열리지 않을 수 있습니다. 본 메일은 발신전용으로 회신이 안되오니 문의사항이 있으시면 삼성증권 Family Center(1588-2323)로 연락하시기 바랍니다. 메일 수신을 원하지 않으시면 수신거부 를 클릭하여 주시기 바랍니다. ▶고객정보변경 06620 서울특별시 서초구 서초대로74길 11 삼성전자빌딩 | 삼성증권주식회사 | 대표이사 박종문 | 사업자등록번호:202-81-48588 COPYRIGHTⓒ SAMSUNG SECURITIES, ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

Title: Using AI to identify hidden talent
Body: Employee concentration, the trade pivot, rewarding talent. - March 31, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/8arCrGZhScF_4CTkEq5UWQ~~/AAQRxRA~/Wm2nciLIY_wyckp9RAxDPia0-Nzuza4brpZk2Z9H60csuWB80tqt5xG3KY_yBWm3jzOzuLvm6Fiq2IsJ8BDRLyz-XIC-stmxzSVz4WGbendSpWeiH4s9wk0sogThC5sWh5UKnOUt1f7otcS22JSFkxlDrYwEJ1OSOGdRltkosCOoIpnHbNB3wQECFf1f1IQypJZdapNCmZydc4LZ_nmmiDa7y4wJzkQZDATp3ZKSEvk~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * Using AI to identify hidden talent * Question of the week * Just for laughs * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/zajt1hn3D254_37bGjY4DQ~~/AAQRxRA~/Wm2nciLIY_wyckp9RAxDPia0-Nzuza4brpZk2Z9H60csuWB80tqt5xG3KY_yBWm3jzOzuLvm6Fiq2IsJ8BDRLyz-XIC-stmxzSVz4WGbendSpWeiH4s9wk0sogThC5sWh5UKnOUt1f7otcS22JSFkyQvBqR5DQkX1g4c0GShfoP5MNY9xLEKYJfWNu7qWw54xiv7WifT06ZgYf9o8wMvZpk31PoHZLvRl_wX3WsMJDc~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/4cyyCQ9pH2YOQLTF6cEZiQ~~/AAQRxRA~/_8_NUxy1A51Gw6sf8RsiY8lhHfRX60rshqhSxnt4hkoTx3ZSuakncpeIRpGOlPavS484v3inqqnuhM9PhsB6PJg7vFQqx_34O9R14M9Hca4605Ifx9deK1uN7dnvozMfiOaG8WsZpZ6A9lGG5co6bVv656BkdlbYrKd5soND1Nk~ ) Workplace trends Education Rising grades, falling standards, and a workplace paying the price Teachers are handing out top grades more freely than ever before, but a new National Bureau of Economic Research study suggests that easier A's are producing less prepared workers. When students receive inflated grades, they are more likely to skip class, score worse on future tests, and earn less over time, the study found. For a typical high school class, the collective hit to future earnings can reach around $213,000. The practice is hard to stop because it keeps everyone happy in the short term — teachers face fewer complaints, parents are pleased, and schools look better on paper. But labor economists warn the real cost shows up years later, when underprepared students enter the workforce. As grades become a weaker proxy for actual ability, HR professionals say the pressure to rethink how candidates are screened is growing. Finances The hidden tax on every employee's concentration Princeton researchers found that financial strain reduces cognitive performance by roughly 13 IQ points, the equivalent of losing a full night's sleep. Workers lose over seven hours of productivity per week to it, costing US employers an estimated $183 billion annually, according to SHRM. The knock-on effects compound: financially stressed employees are twice as likely to be job hunting, per PwC, and cost employers around $400 more per year in healthcare. HR experts argue that as housing, childcare, and healthcare costs continue to outpace wages, workplace financial wellbeing is shifting from a nice-to-have to a measurable business issue. The AI corner Careers Young workers are making big bets to stay ahead of AI A growing number of young people are overhauling their career plans before AI can do it for them. Enrollment at vocational community colleges has grown nearly 20% since 2020, per National Student Clearinghouse data, as workers pivot to trades they believe are harder to automate. Stanford University research found that employment among workers aged 22 to 25 in AI-exposed roles declined 16% since late 2022. A Harvard survey found 59% of Americans aged 18 to 29 see AI as a threat to their job prospects, with many either fleeing vulnerable fields or doubling down on AI skills to stay ahead. The WSJ reports it is a generation placing calculated bets on where human work will still matter. Talent Top researchers are leaving universities for tech companies A new University of Chicago study found that more than two-thirds of AI researchers now work in the private sector, up from less than half in 2001, driven by salaries that have more than tripled while academic pay has barely moved. The consequences go beyond universities — when researchers move to industry, they publish less and patent more, meaning knowledge that was once shared freely becomes locked behind corporate walls. AI labs are also expanding their hiring well beyond scientists, competing for top economists, policy experts, and communications professionals. The Financial Times compares it to the pre-2008 banking boom, when a generation of talent was pulled away from public institutions toward finance. Using AI to identify hidden talent By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/iZDSrk2wvQV5dvQOp_Mm-Q~~/AAQRxRA~/Wm2nciLIY_wyckp9RAxDPia0-Nzuza4brpZk2Z9H60csuWB80tqt5xG3KY_yBWm3jzOzuLvm6Fiq2IsJ8BDRLyz-XIC-stmxzSVz4WGbendSpWeiH4s9wk0sogThC5sWh5UKnOUt1f7otcS22JSFkwcyruUGcoceIqS1HsxVPRxbWrEKZzjqsP1FvoiP1-Tm0jinqRndIy3LzM0R9WOVb084ilglOnXufB6smw1-bbs~ ) In many teams, performance visibility isn’t evenly distributed. The people who speak up most in meetings or stay closest to leadership often get the most recognition, while others who consistently deliver high-quality work can go unnoticed. The performance visibility gap is widening as more work happens across tools and asynchronously. Contributions live in project boards, comments, documents, and messages, not just in meetings or presentations. As a result, it’s hard to see who’s truly performing when you have to rely on observation alone. By analyzing patterns across your team’s workflows, AI can help you understand who is contributing consistently, where work is moving forward, and which employees are quietly making a difference over time. It gives you a broader view of performance that goes beyond visibility and into actual contribution. Of course, the goal isn’t to replace your judgment; it’s to make sure you’re seeing the full picture before you use it. So how can you use AI to better identify high performers and uncover hidden talent? Look for patterns Consistency over time is often a stronger signal of performance than any single result. So start pulling data from your project management tool, including task completion, deadlines met, or project ownership. Then, export the data and use an AI tool to analyze it. Ask the AI to identify which team members consistently meet deadlines, take on high-priority work, or contribute across multiple projects. You might also compare planned timelines versus actual delivery to see who is reliably executing. This information gives you a more complete view of how work is getting done over time, not just what’s most visible in the moment. Key question: “Which performers are quietly but constantly delivering?” Identify the people behind team success Big outcomes are rarely the result of a single person’s effort, yet recognition often centers on the most visible contributor. That can leave important work invisible. After a project wraps, think about using AI to review meeting notes and team communication. Prompt it to summarize who contributed and how, whether that’s coordinating work, solving key problems, or keeping things moving. AI note-taking tools can also help capture who is contributing ideas and decisions throughout meetings, not just who presents at the end. This helps you build a more accurate picture of how success actually happens on your team and ensures recognition reflects reality. Key question: “Who is contributing to your team’s biggest wins that might not be getting credit today?” Look for growth Top performers aren’t always the ones with the highest output today. Sometimes the most important signal is momentum; who is improving, taking on more responsibility, or expanding their impact. You can use AI to compare performance data over time, such as task ownership, project complexity, or feedback from one-on-ones. Ask your AI tool to identify trends over the past few months and highlight who is progressing quickly. You can also input notes from 1:1s or past reviews and have AI surface patterns in how an employee’s role or contributions are evolving. Spotting emerging talent earlier will help you support those employees more intentionally. Key question: “Who on your team is growing quickly, even if they’re not your top performer yet?” Challenge your assumptions Every manager develops a mental model of their team, but those perceptions are naturally incomplete. AI can act as a second perspective. Before finalizing evaluations, compare your own assessment with AI-driven insights. For example, you might share your ranking of team performance and ask AI to identify any discrepancies based on available data. It can highlight where someone’s output doesn’t match your perception or where consistent contributors may be under-recognized. Remember not to defer to the AI tool; use it to pressure-test your thinking and catch blind spots. Key question: “Where might your assumptions about performance be incomplete or outdated?” Put those insights in motion Identifying talent is only useful if it leads to better decisions. Once you have a clearer view of performance, use AI to help you act on it. You can ask AI to draft recognition messages that reflect specific contributions, suggest stretch assignments based on someone’s strengths, or outline development plans for employees who are trending upward. It can also help you track who has been recognized recently to ensure appreciation is distributed fairly. These small actions reinforce that great work is seen and create more opportunities for people to grow. Key question: “How are you turning performance insights into meaningful recognition and growth opportunities?” Water cooler chatter A man from Florida used ChatGPT to sell his house. He walked away with $100,000 more than any real estate agent had estimated, closing the deal in five days. Using the AI to handle pricing, marketing, and negotiations, he found it gave him more confidence than the professionals he consulted. "It doesn't necessarily replace professionals. But it does allow us all to have the ability to be more curious and to feel more confident in the decisions we're making.'" Robert Levine, Home Seller A British retailer is selling a wooden "influencer kit" for toddlers. The kit is complete with a miniature camera, tripod, smartphone, and microphone - and not everyone is charmed. The £15 toy, aimed at children as young as two, has sparked debate about whether influencing should sit alongside doctor sets and toy kitchens as aspirational career play. "Influencing is all about chasing attention, so we have to ask what we're teaching children to value." Daisy Greenwell, Co-founder, Smartphone Free Childhood Question of the week Last week’s answer: 74% This week’s question: What percentage of job seekers have been ghosted by an employer in the past year? Title: [삼성증권] 모니모 금융거래정보 이용 및 제공 안내
Body: 오픈뱅킹 금융거래정보 제공사실 안내 [삼성증권] 모니모 금융거래정보 이용 · 제공 안내 안훈규 고객님, 모니모 앱을 통한 금융거래정보 제공 동의에 따라 아래와 같이 고객님의 정보가 제공됐습니다. 「금융실명거래 및 비밀보장에 관한 법률」 제4조의2에 따른 내용입니다. ■ 제공받는 자 : 삼성생명, 삼성화재, 삼성카드 ■ 제공일 : 2026년 03월 30일 ■ 사용 목적 ① 모니모 내에서 제공(링크를 통한 제휴사 연결방식 포함)되는 - 삼성카드, 삼성생명, 삼성화재, 삼성증권 상품 · 서비스 및 각종 제휴 서비스 이용권유 및 판촉활동 - 편의사항 (사은품, 할인쿠폰, 캐쉬백, 마일리지 등) 안내, 맞춤형 서비스 제공, 시장조사, 경품제공, 상품 · 서비스 개발 및 연구 ② 삼성카드, 삼성생명, 삼성화재, 삼성증권 각 사가 기 보유중인 개인(신용)정보를 결합하여 고객정보관리, 고객통계 및 분석 - ②의 결과를 활용하여, 모니모 외에서 제공되는 삼성카드, 삼성생명, 삼성화재, 삼성증권 각 사의 (1) 상품 · 서비스 안내 및 이용권유 (2) 편의사항(사은품 · 경품, 할인쿠폰 등) 안내 (3) 상품 · 서비스 연구 개발 ■ 주요 내용 · 전일 잔고 및 계좌 보유 현황(예수금, 주식 및 금융상품 보유 현황 등 포함) · 최근 1년 주식 및 금융상품 거래내역(거래채널, 거래금액, 거래빈도 등) · 최근 3개월 신용 · 대출 현황 및 미수동결계좌 현황 등 금융거래 정보 해당 정보는 관련 법률에 따라, 1회에 한해 제공됐습니다. 신청 내용과 다르거나 문의사항이 있는 경우 모니모 고객센터 로 문의바랍니다. 감사합니다. 본 메일은 삼성증권에서 발송한 발신전용 메일입니다. 보다 자세한 문의사항은 삼성증권 Family Center(1588-2323)로 연락하시기 바랍니다. ▶고객정보변경 서울특별시 서초구 서초대로74길 11 삼성전자빌딩 | 삼성증권주식회사 | 대표이사 사장 박종문 | 사업자등록번호:202-81-48588 COPYRIGHTⓒ SAMSUNG SECURITIES, ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

Title: [삼성증권] 고객님, 퇴직연금 부담금납입내역안내 입니다.
Body: 보안메일 보안메일 저희 삼성증권에서는 고객님의 개인정보 보안을 위하여 금융거래정보 조회에 보안메일 기능을 이용하고 있습니다. 첨부파일을 클릭하시고 비밀번호를 입력해주시기 바랍니다. STEP 1 첨부파일 열기 STEP 2 비밀번호 입력 STEP 3 금융거래 조회 생년월일(YYMMDD) 6자리 또는 당사에 등록한 고객번호(사업자등록번호 또는 투자등록번호 등) 전체 일부 메일계정에서는 첨부파일 바로 열기가 지원되지 않을 수 있습니다. 이러한 경우 첨부를 저장한 후 실행하십시오. 회사에서 사용하시는 PC의 경우 회사 내 보안 정책으로 인하여 보안메일이 열리지 않을 수 있습니다. 본 메일은 삼성증권에서 발송한 발신전용 메일입니다. 보다 자세한 문의사항은 삼성증권 Family Center(1588-2323)로 연락하시기 바랍니다. ▶고객정보변경 06620 서울특별시 서초구 서초대로74길 11 삼성전자빌딩 | 삼성증권주식회사 | 대표이사 박종문 | 사업자등록번호:202-81-48588 COPYRIGHTⓒ SAMSUNG SECURITIES, ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

Title: Try AI in Confluence today with Premium
Body: Atlassian ✨ Unlock knowledge, accelerate work, and turn insights into action with AI Are you as excited about AI as we are? If so, we can't wait for you to try AI in Confluence Premium. It transforms teamwork with the power of AI‑human collaboration, empowering each team member to do their best work so that the entire team can accomplish more together. >> https://support.atlassian.com/organization-administration/docs/atlassian-intelligence-features-in-confluence/?utm_source=sfmc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-2A_EML-20696&jobid=107752489&subid=1897115151&send_date=3/25/2026 Using AI‑powered capabilities within Confluence, teams can now: 🕙 Save time and get up to speed quickly by summarizing long‑winded content or meeting notes. ✏️ Generate new content, such as strategy pages and project overviews, with a simple prompt. 👥 Change tone, improve writing and make technical information easier for other teams to understand. ⚙️ Easily create automation rules by simply describing what you'd like to automate and let AI create the rule for you. Try AI >> https://metalogos.atlassian.net/admin/billing/applications/change-edition/confluence.ondemand?atlOrigin=eyJpIjoiNmYyNWRlMGMtMTgyOC00N2Q1LThjMGYtMjM0NTFjYTFiOTQxIiwicCI6InNmbWMifQ== Want to explore what other features you'll unlock with Confluence Premium? Title: Maximizing a multi-gen team
Body: Female CEOs, what Gen Z is missing, teams working together. - March 24, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/HKBhPl8nEypI-n72scGjvg~~/AAQRxRA~/IggUxBUGFTU-iejG6hOgDs_FNF-sb6aHQnyaXCKBkkI37kiorSGX14HzQaQf_NMOPPL4WT0FgtLiJmYoR1sQMjRs2jIfQ0EAFDdqXXXnXDF_dO8cM9KcS0MPHNOHPYBOOLur2R3MJeYH9ebRiM861doOAw1wAeWibi-sv7tiYG9IC5v2gqDpply1hEv9QbawrKMk4oDn8aQf2yLFt38auLEUy86a-QYHUipt48D34j0~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * Maximizing a multi-generational team * Question of the week * Just for laughs * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/KMDMC0oKvnc0_4PDuNrZkQ~~/AAQRxRA~/IggUxBUGFTU-iejG6hOgDs_FNF-sb6aHQnyaXCKBkkI37kiorSGX14HzQaQf_NMOPPL4WT0FgtLiJmYoR1sQMjRs2jIfQ0EAFDdqXXXnXDF_dO8cM9KcS0MPHNOHPYBOOLur2R3MJeYH9ebRiM861VJ_k3fkUe5O46j16yhx-apHsAqCx3_wl_P0kwqDZJEXMpPT4XsIiuvdVczfUuNrNj2cDl57rVp9EJH53Vd8Na4~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/KqQdjIw3ZhmeKLRg7eh4bw~~/AAQRxRA~/IY0YuGPblCD8eNhEZps_ST95OhFwblr0NecZCeaQeJYrHBkJFRAyAY6LVKSBZ4Y9z1vymtHXe31lMHg7JwDqVWjuJpcAi0lQzIKCTf6ytDpHOJGm9LibRBcmVYnF8XUkb6UK4iT--0zcmx2eNqw1HXFpvaAvC6oBfpu21w6IwCY~ ) Workplace trends Pay Having a female boss could close the gender pay gap Companies led by female CEOs had a gender pay gap 4.3 percentage points smaller than those led by men, according to an analysis of more than 40 employers by the Global 50/50 think-tank. Analysts suggest female leaders are more likely to evaluate men and women's potential evenly, and that women feel more confident negotiating salaries when the person at the top looks like them. The catch: female CEOs in the 500 biggest US companies still account for just 11% of the total, according to the Financial Times, making the benefit hard to access for most. Until that changes, the pay gap - currently around 77 cents for every dollar a man earns - is unlikely to close anytime soon. Relationships Why dating less is making Gen Z worse at work Only 56% of Gen Z enter adulthood having been in a relationship, compared to 75% of older generations, according to the Survey Center on American Life. Research by a psychology professor at New York University finds a direct link between that decline and workplace performance - skills like negotiation, compromise, and handling difficult conversations carry over directly from personal to professional life. With Gen Z set to make up nearly 30% of the US workforce by 2030, per the Bureau of Labor Statistics. Experts say the fix requires effort on both sides - managers need to make unspoken workplace norms explicit, while younger workers need to embrace the discomfort of learning them. The AI corner Education AI is making it easier than ever to cheat on your MBA Unlike traditional classrooms where professors can monitor behavior, online environments give students near-invisible cover to misuse AI, according to the Financial Times. Detection software, including widely used platforms like Turnitin and GPTZero, has proven largely ineffective, struggling to keep pace with rapidly evolving AI models. Making matters worse, AI can write essays in a student's own tone of voice, making misconduct even harder to confirm, says the director of academic environment at Warwick Business School. Schools are responding by shifting toward oral exams and group projects, but the deeper fix has to be cultural, argues the head of school at AGSM, students only grow when they wrestle with hard problems, not when they outsource them. Authenticity The race to prove your work is human-made At least eight separate initiatives are now competing to create a universally recognised "human-made" label, similar to the Fair Trade logo, according to the BBC. Publishers, filmmakers, and advertisers are already adopting stamps like "Proudly Human" and "No AI" as both a marketing tool and a response to growing consumer distrust. The challenge is agreeing on a definition - AI is now so embedded in everyday tools that drawing a clean line between human and machine work is complicated, says Sasha Luccioni, an AI research scientist. Without a single standard, experts warn consumers risk being left more confused than reassured. Maximizing a multi-generational team By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/a5Jm6-Z1oxVugbtO3_VtAw~~/AAQRxRA~/IggUxBUGFTU-iejG6hOgDs_FNF-sb6aHQnyaXCKBkkI37kiorSGX14HzQaQf_NMOPPL4WT0FgtLiJmYoR1sQMjRs2jIfQ0EAFDdqXXXnXDF_dO8cM9KcS0MPHNOHPYBOOLur2R3MJeYH9ebRiM861ZQmerYaKAfYUQYtvXB5GEc4WeRI7QLUJIFI2hggdXJYOqoGjGXRLx-iisU_YVdAL5VmjLXyDoFZIwX1nfRWrpc~ ) Having a multi-generational team is a huge advantage. Research from People Management shows that diverse teams are 87% better at decision-making, and McKinsey reports they are 35–39% more likely to outperform financially. Generational diversity can be a major driver of these benefits because people who grew up in different eras tend to approach problems from different angles, leading to stronger and more innovative ideas. Each generation also brings unique strengths, from relationship-building to technological fluency to bold, fresh thinking. Of course, navigating generational differences can be challenging. Varying communication styles, expectations around work-life balance, and comfort with new technology can create friction if they’re not addressed. As a leader, you can create the conditions where each generation contributes its strengths and learns from the others. The goal isn’t to smooth out differences; it’s to use them to your team’s advantage. So how can you maximize the strengths of a multi-generational workforce? Pair experience with fresh perspectives One of the greatest advantages of a multi-generational team is the combination of long-term experience and new ways of thinking. Employees who have spent years in an industry often recognize patterns, risks, and opportunities that others might miss. At the same time, newer professionals often question long-standing assumptions and introduce new approaches to solving problems. Try intentionally bringing these perspectives together by pairing employees with different levels of experience on projects. For example, you might pair a big-picture-thinking Baby Boomer with an AI-savvy Gen Zer on a project focused on building and scaling customer relationships. When teams collaborate across generations, ideas are both creative and grounded in real-world context, often leading to stronger outcomes. Key question: “Which team members can I pair to blend experience with fresh thinking?” Try a knowledge exchange Different generations often bring different areas of expertise to the workplace. Some employees may have deep subject-matter knowledge, strong client relationships, or strategic insight built over years of experience. Others may be highly skilled in emerging tools, digital workflows, or new ways of collaborating. Encourage your team to learn from one another by creating opportunities for knowledge exchange. This could include informal mentoring, project presentations, or short “show-and-tell” sessions where employees share how they approach their work. When people see that everyone has something valuable to teach, generational differences start to feel like a resource rather than a divide. Key question: “Where can my team create more opportunities for knowledge sharing?” Let each generation shape communication Communication styles often vary across generations. Some employees prefer quick digital messages, while others lean toward more structured conversations or meetings. Rather than forcing one style on the entire team, use these differences to improve clarity and collaboration. Define when each type of communication is most effective. For example, quick updates like project statuses might live in a designated messaging channel, while strategy sessions require a meeting. By blending different approaches, teams often create systems that are both efficient and thoughtful. Key question: “How can your team combine different communication styles to work more effectively together?” Use AI as a bridge between generations New AI technologies are changing how work gets done, and employees across generations may approach these tools differently. Some team members may quickly experiment with AI and automation, while others may take a more cautious approach and want to understand how it fits into their workflow. You can turn this difference into a learning opportunity. Encourage those experimenting with AI to share their discoveries, while inviting more experienced employees to guide where these tools should be applied strategically. When teams combine curiosity with seasoned judgment, AI becomes a powerful support system rather than a source of division. Key question: “How can you combine experimentation and experience to use AI more effectively as a team?” Water cooler chatter Wired headphones are back. Revenue jumped 20% in the first six weeks of 2026, according to analytics firm Circana, as consumers ditch Bluetooth in favor of cables. The trend is part style statement, part tech backlash as dangling wires have become fashion, with many crediting the rise of AI with making them nostalgic for simpler technology. "It feels like a lot of people are almost turning on technology because it's becoming so advanced. I think there's a general group mindset where we're like, 'I don't like how this feels.'" Aryn Grusin, social worker A CEO went viral for eating his own burger like he'd never seen one before. When McDonald's chief executive posted an awkward taste test on social media, the internet erupted - memes multiplied, and the clip became a symbol of the gap between corner-office culture and the real world. The unexpected twist: sales went up, proving that in today's workplace, even a cringe moment can be good for business, according to Axios. "Virality is a product of putting a lot of shots on goal. Kempczinski has been showing up day after day, week after week, for years on social media." Sara Wilson, founder, SW Projects Question of the week Last week’s answer: 39% This week’s question: What percentage of small businesses struggle to find candidates with AI skills? Title: Ubisoft 계정: 개인정보 설정 관리
Body: Ubisoft 계정: 개인정보 설정 관리 ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ https://view.updates.ubisoft.com/?%3D 브라우저에서 보기 https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? 안녕하세요, 님. 저희 커뮤니티의 일원이 되어 주심에 감사드리며, 저희를 신뢰해 주셔서 감사합니다. 저희가 게임을 만드는 즐거움만큼 플레이어님께서도 즐겁게 게임을 즐기고 계시길 바랍니다. Ubisoft는 특별한 세계를 만드는 것뿐만 아니라, 여러분의 개인정보를 보호하고 프라이버시와 관련된 선택을 존중하는 것도 우선순위로 여기고 있습니다. 플레이어의 자유로운 선택을 위해 https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? 개인정보 센터 를 마련했습니다. 계정 설정에서 이용 가능한 모든 개인화 옵션은 플레이어의 프라이버시를 존중하고 보호하도록 설계되었습니다. 예를 들어, 뉴스레터와 Ubisoft 및 파트너사의 웹사이트와 애플리케이션에서의 맞춤형 광고, 소셜 설정에 대한 기본 설정을 언제든지 업데이트하실 수 있습니다. 계정에 로그인하여 "계정 관리" 탭에서 개인정보 보호 센터에 접속하시기만 하면 됩니다. 보안 강화를 위해 https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? 2단계 인증 을 활성화하여 게임과 개인 데이터를 무단 접근으로부터 보호하는 것을 강력히 권장합니다. 지금 바로 확인하고 계정을 설정해 보세요! 또한 정기적으로 업데이트되는 https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? 개인정보 보호정책 에서 UBISOFT가 여러분의 데이터를 어떻게 처리하는지 확인하실 수 있습니다. 더 멋진 게임이 당신을 기다립니다. 함께 즐겨보세요! https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? ©2026 Ubisoft Entertainment. Title: (광고) SouthernCros 님, 오버워치 x 프로젝트 요르하 콜라보를 놓치지 마세요!
Body:
프로젝트 요르하 콜라보가 추가되고, 신화 메이 스킨이 화제에 오르며, 1시즌 진영 이벤트 보상이 배틀 패스에서 기다립니다!
18175
모두의 영광을 위해
SouthernCros 님, 이것은 저주일까요, 아니면 벌일까요?

3월 11일부터 24일까지, 오버워치 x 프로젝트 요르하가 뭉쳐 오버워치 영웅들에게 한층 냉혹한 갈등이 피어나는 의상을 제공합니다. 이번 콜라보레이션에서는 어두운 SF와 비장한 결의가 결합해 의무, 희생, 생존을 형상화한 실루엣을 선보입니다.
다섯 가지 신규 전설 스킨으로 압박감 속에서도 목적 의식을 잃지 않고 더 나은 미래를 위해 함께 싸우는 요르하의 대표 안드로이드들을 전장에 불러 보세요. 모두의 영광을 위해 싸우는 스킨 목록은 다음과 같습니다.
  • 2B 키리코
  • 9S 우양
  • A2 벤데타
  • 사령관 화이트 메르시
  • 아담 라이프위버
스킨은 개별 묶음 상품이나 대형 묶음 상품의 구성으로 판매됩니다. 콜라보 테마 로비로 출격 전의 분위기를 한껏 느껴 보세요. 이벤트 도전 과제를 완료하면 프로필 카드, 플레이어 칭호, 특별 무기 장식품 등 다양한 꾸미기 아이템을 획득할 수 있으니, 모두 콜라보레이션 이벤트 허브에서 확인해 보세요.

밝은 미래는 주어지는 것이 아니라, 싸워서 쟁취해야 합니다. 오버워치 x 프로젝트 요르하 콜라보레이션과 오늘 함께하세요!
지금 출격하기
OWCS 천국의 수호자 대회 후원 묶음 상품을 확인하세요!
천국의 수호자 대형 묶음 상품을 구매하여 OWCS 챔피언스 클래시 우승 상금에 직접 기여하고, 최고의 무대에서 겨루는 팀들의 치열한 승부를 지원하세요.

이 묶음 상품에는 우양, 라인하르트, 라이프위버 전설 스킨이 포함되어 있으며, 응원하는 마음을 게임 내에서 표현할 수 있는 멋진 기회를 제공합니다. 각 스킨은 별도로 구매해 챔피언스 클래시 우승 상금에 기여할 수도 있습니다.

장비를 챙기고, 가장 좋아하는 플레이어를 응원하고, 다음 경기에서 이 전설적인 모습을 자랑해 보세요.
지금 구매하기
방송 시작! 메이로 채팅창을 얼려 버리세요
신화 방송 시작! 메이로 화려하고 빛나는 매력을 뽐내며 스포트라이트를 한껏 받는 스트리머 판타지에 빠져들어 보세요. 그렇다고 귀여운 토끼에 너무 헤벌쭉하진 마세요. 큰코다칠 수 있거든요! 전투는 더 귀엽게, 처치는 더 차갑게, 그리고 구독과 좋아요 꼭 눌러 주세요!

신화 스킨으로 방송을 시작한 다음, 추가 헤어스타일, 헤드폰 스타일, 장밋빛 핑크와 강렬한 퍼플 펑크 배색, 픽셀 기반 공격을 강화해 주는 기술 시각 효과 전환, 빛나는 에너지로 분위기를 끌어올리는 주변 스프라이트 효과를 활용해 메이를 궁극의 아이돌로 완성시키세요.
방송 시작하기
멋지게 진영을 선택하세요
1시즌 프리미엄 배틀 패스로 오버워치의 새로운 제국과 시대 속 나만의 자리를 반영한 보상을 얻으세요. 진영을 테마로 한 꾸미기 아이템, 다섯 개의 전설 스킨, 500 크레딧 등 의미 있는 보상이 단계마다 기다립니다. 추가로 신화 프리즘을 최대 80개 획득해서 신화 메르시 천상의 수호자, 신화 방송 시작! 메이, 또는 주노의 스타 슈터 무기를 얻으세요!

궁극의 배틀 패스를 확보하고 프리미엄 트랙의 모든 콘텐츠와 20단계 건너뛰기, 2,000 오버워치 코인, 새로운 시대 소전과 새로운 제국 엠레 전설 스킨도 손에 넣어 보세요.
준비 완료
새로운 제트팩 캣 스킨으로 날아오르세요
1시즌 스타터 팩에는 프리미엄 배틀 패스나 새로운 상점 스킨을 구매하기에 충분한 1,000 오버워치 코인이 포함되어 있으며, 전투기 조종사 제트팩 캣 특급 스킨도 귀여운 보너스로 제공됩니다. 지금 오버워치 상점으로 달려가 이륙을 준비하세요!
지금 구매하기
소셜 서비스
인재 채용 | 회사 소개 | 개인정보 처리방침 | 법률 관련 | 고객지원
오버워치 관련 최신 소식, 특별 할인, 베타 및 기타 사항 안내 이메일에 관심이 없다면 수신 거부로 설정해 주시기 바랍니다. 모든 이메일을 수신하고 싶지 않다면 구독 해지를 클릭하세요. 전반적인 환경 설정은 이곳을 클릭하여 조정할 수 있습니다.
