Title: Helping your team manage their energy
Body: The cost of task-switching, AI-surveys & small-biz benefits. - March 17, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/zMwxdUEgn1qP4TMKz4Bpfw~~/AAQRxRA~/JzNJBjwu8cL4C1qPNyeFHikfYbeVnE524ZwWgr5sA0Az7LWwQDZRIM5I1QKW8s0dLo0qeSturtKICLZhKn8OMPd6sTvDnhtSG4_L8PGbj4FpVQw7UcWifIiUYdrliDzUerG8nIp7z66qboRK6ZPG7Xb4zeVxNaNtJQWdCYCdZlny4cfWlArYTTvo0t-OpYaXNbAs2L1AyD_fBVPIN59kfXUOGWV6k5MT2S52g-M0RY0~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * Helping your team manage their energy * Question of the week * Just for laughs * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/KRPUANs28sS5RvuN5r9saA~~/AAQRxRA~/JzNJBjwu8cL4C1qPNyeFHikfYbeVnE524ZwWgr5sA0Az7LWwQDZRIM5I1QKW8s0dLo0qeSturtKICLZhKn8OMPd6sTvDnhtSG4_L8PGbj4FpVQw7UcWifIiUYdrliDzUerG8nIp7z66qboRK6ZPG7T8YxlmJuPq-FmUpUeme9VCLvcD-IvmAwoEbbl7JWIDh7GkxOIGtNXusScdT0pTREjRWfPjQFWDiba04bDriWvc~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/LObVB2IE0MLFuHu21oHlXQ~~/AAQRxRA~/Kg21vEl-ICB-v-V27h5dFLfVURkxp0v2pDxtSoQhQ9L-W5rIYjwAaSptDeGAPOdXWF0bkYIO8kxxN6Wg4kMq1BY8OwpZL2L1ZvBzU1tc4iP2-vvIqp4j1l3oU2DuHOF2XWr9LJzL4buVmeiNw08p8laZyuCaEd5LnxGKjrvyueI~ ) Workplace trends Remote Work Working from home may be the most affordable fertility policy Governments have tried cash bonuses, tax breaks, and state-sponsored matchmaking to reverse falling birth rates - but new research suggests the answer may be closer to home. A study drawing on data from 38 countries, funded by the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development, found that fertility was 14% higher among couples where both partners worked from home at least once a week. Flexibility over where one works is among the most promising and cost-effective ways to help people have the families they want, according to researchers from King's College London, Stanford, and Princeton. Remote work cuts commuting, makes school pickups manageable, and gives parents control over their day in a way the traditional nine-to-five doesn't allow. Benefits Small businesses are finally catching up on retirement plans The share of small US businesses offering retirement plans has jumped from 19% to 30% in just six years, according to a recent report by payroll platform Gusto. Two things are driving the shift: more than 20 states now require employers to automatically enroll staff in retirement accounts, and a competitive job market pushed smaller businesses to step up their benefits game. The tight labor market of recent years created a real incentive for smaller employers to add perks that were once considered out of reach, and retirement plans rose to the top of that list. There's a strong link between the availability of these plans and employee retention, according to a Gusto economist - making retirement benefits one of the more straightforward investments a small business can make in its workforce. The AI corner Research AI is replacing the people companies used to survey Companies have spent decades paying consultants and running focus groups to understand their customers - but AI is now offering a faster, cheaper alternative. A new crop of startups is building AI-powered digital replicas of real individuals, trained on their preferences and behaviors, that companies can query instead of running traditional research. The process that once took months can now take minutes, at a fraction of the cost, according to the Wall Street Journal. CVS Health has already put the technology to work, finding its AI-simulated respondents replicated known research findings with up to 95% accuracy. Gallup is also piloting the approach for policy and trend research. Finance Millions are turning to AI for retirement advice - with mixed results Managing a pension has never been straightforward, but a growing number of people are skipping the financial adviser entirely and turning to AI instead, according to the Financial Times. A survey of 5,000 people commissioned by Lloyds Banking Group found that more than half of adults now use AI platforms for financial advice. The appeal is obvious - availability around the clock, no sales pressure, and free. But experts warn the technology has real blind spots: AI tools have been found to give outdated or outright wrong advice on complex tax and pension rules, and the confidence with which they provide answers can make errors hard to spot. Helping your team manage their energy By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/mrpVjcMcRPqlHi4dGO6r9A~~/AAQRxRA~/JzNJBjwu8cL4C1qPNyeFHikfYbeVnE524ZwWgr5sA0Az7LWwQDZRIM5I1QKW8s0dLo0qeSturtKICLZhKn8OMPd6sTvDnhtSG4_L8PGbj4FpVQw7UcWifIiUYdrliDzUerG8nIp7z66qboRK6ZPG7WUT6S9kvSvt3vxMPy3d1RCiWw7rBnNYYb7loSRsclvCYVUlxQ8FBjIk4NVcBfV1BueinN8mdVVGlFahCexbIQw~ ) Productivity isn’t just about having time. It’s about having mental energy. When employees juggle a mix of creative and technical responsibilities or constantly jump between meetings and execution, they’re forced to context-switch several times a day. Maybe your team members move from solving complex problems to presenting ideas, navigating shifting priorities, responding to a steady stream of messages, and attending meetings. That kind of mental fragmentation makes it difficult to sustain focus and creative thinking. Research from the American Psychological Association has found that switching between tasks can cost up to 40% of productive time. Another study published in PMC shows that frequent task switching can lead to cognitive overload, mental fatigue, and increased stress. In other words, even when people are busy all day, constant switching can quietly drain the energy they need to do their best work. As a leader, one of the most valuable things you can do is help your team manage their energy, not just their time. When work rhythms support both focus and recovery, employees are far more likely to sustain high performance without burning out. So how can you help your team protect their energy and stay effective? Look for energy signals High performers are often the least likely to say they’re overwhelmed; they’ll deliver results and may quietly absorb additional work until their energy is depleted. So try to watch for the signals of cognitive overload. Maybe someone who is normally decisive starts second-guessing small decisions. Maybe a team member who is usually enthusiastic becomes quiet in meetings or takes longer to respond to messages. Regular one-on-one conversations can help uncover these signals before they become bigger problems, so instead of just asking about project status, ask questions about workload, like which parts of your work are requiring the most mental energy right now? Where are you feeling the most friction this week? When you make capacity part of the conversation, employees feel more comfortable sharing what they actually need to stay effective. Key question: “What signals might tell you that someone on your team is experiencing cognitive overload?” Create clear priority filters Uncertainty drains energy fast. When employees are juggling multiple requests, shifting priorities, and receiving constant messages, they can end up spending more energy deciding what to do than actually doing the work. You can help by creating clear priority filters. Define the top two or three outcomes the team is focused on for the quarter, or outline how to evaluate incoming requests. When employees understand the bigger picture, it becomes easier to decide where to invest their effort. You might also encourage team members to periodically review their tasks and ask whether each one contributes directly to the team’s goals. If something doesn’t clearly support those priorities, it may be worth reconsidering or simplifying. Key question: “What simple priority filters could help your team focus their energy on the work that matters most?” Design more sustainable work rhythms Structure is crucial for maximizing energy. Help your employees design work rhythms that protect focus time. Maybe that means clustering meetings into specific blocks of the day, so employees can use that other time to concentrate on deeper work. Even small adjustments like shortening recurring meetings or clarifying agendas can reduce unnecessary mental load. It can also help to normalize the idea that not every moment of the workday needs to be filled. Strategic pauses between projects or meetings give people time to think, process information, and prepare for what’s next. When work rhythms support focus and recovery, employees can sustain their energy and performance far more effectively. Key question: “How could you structure your team’s week to create more protected time for focused work?” Use AI to reduce low-value drain Many of the tasks that consume energy today aren’t the work people find most meaningful. Things like summarizing meetings, organizing notes, drafting routine updates, or sorting through long threads can quietly eat up hours of mental attention. This is where AI can help. Encourage your employees to use tools that summarize conversations, extract action items, or generate first drafts to reduce cognitive friction from the day. Challenge your team to experiment with using AI for these kinds of tasks so they can reserve more of their energy for creative thinking, strategic decisions, and collaboration. For example, someone might use AI to summarize a long project discussion before reviewing it themselves, or to turn a rough outline into a draft they can refine. When AI handles the first layer of routine work, employees can spend their energy where it matters most. Key question: “Which tasks on your team consume energy but add relatively little strategic value?” Water cooler chatter More women are out-earning their partners, and relationships are feeling the strain. The share of women who are the primary or sole breadwinner in opposite-sex marriages has tripled over 50 years. But research suggests the shift is causing tension between spouses, not regarding who earns more money but revolving around who holds higher professional status according to the Financial Times. "What matters isn't just income, but occupational prestige." Kaisa Snellman, Professor of Organisational Behaviour, Insead Gen Z is bringing the mall back. The generation that grew up online is spending a greater share of their dollars in physical stores than older generations, with 62% of their general merchandise purchases made in person, according to the Wall Street Journal. Malls are responding by redesigning common areas for impromptu photoshoots and courting online-only brands to open physical locations. "I don't really like online shopping. I'm an instant-gratification girl. I need it now." Savera Ghorzang, Gen Z Shopper Question of the week Last week’s answer: 36% This week’s question: What percentage of employees have cried at least once in the workplace? Title: [SW Expert Academy] 휴면 계정 안내
Body: 휴면 계정 전환 완료 안내 - SW Expert Academy 휴면 계정 전환 완료 안내 안녕하세요. SW Expert Academy입니다. 개인정보보호를 위해 관계 법령에 따라 회원님의 개인정보가 별도 분리저장되어 아이디가 휴면 계정으로 전환 되었습니다. 회원 계정 dksgnsrb@gmail.com 휴면 전환일 2026년 03월 17일 분리보관 정보 회원정보 및 서비스 신청시 입력한 개인정보 ※ 상시 SW 역량테스트의 경우 유효기간 2년에 따라 휴면계정 전환 후 2년간 저장/관리 전환 사유 마지막 로그인 이력을 기준으로 1년간 미사용 관계 법령 - 정보통신망 이용촉진 및 정보보호 등에 관한 법률 제29조 - 정보통신망 이용촉진 및 정보보호 등에 관한 법률 시행령 제16조 SW Expert Academy 바로가기 본 메일은 발신 전용이므로 회신 되지 않습니다. 문의사항은 홈페이지의 Q&A 를 이용해 주시기 바랍니다. ⓒ 2017 SAMSUNG. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED.

Title: 안훈규 고객님의 2026년 03월 요금명세서입니다.
Body: New Templage 2026.03 고객님의 2026 년 03 월 요금내역입니다. 이 명세서는 고객님의 정보보안을 위해 암호화되어 있습니다. 본 메일은 발신전용메일입니다. 회신이 되지않습니다. 문의사항이 있는 경우, 현대자동차 고객센터(080-600-6000)로 연락주시기 바랍니다. ※ 현대자동차 고객센터 이용 시간 : 평일 오전 9시~오후 6시 서울특별시 서초구 헌릉로 12 (양재동) | 현대자동차 고객센터 080-600-6000 All rights reserved. ⓒ 2014 Hyundai Motor Company

Title: (광고) SouthernCros 님, 오버워치 x 프로젝트 요르하 콜라보가 출격합니다!
Body:
프로젝트 요르하 콜라보가 추가되고, 신화 메이 스킨이 화제에 오르며, 1시즌 진영 이벤트 보상이 배틀 패스에서 기다립니다!
18175
모두의 영광을 위해
SouthernCros 님, 이것은 저주일까요, 아니면 벌일까요?

3월 11일부터 24일까지, 오버워치 x 프로젝트 요르하가 뭉쳐 오버워치 영웅들에게 한층 냉혹한 갈등이 피어나는 의상을 제공합니다. 이번 콜라보레이션에서는 어두운 SF와 비장한 결의가 결합해 의무, 희생, 생존을 형상화한 실루엣을 선보입니다.
다섯 가지 신규 전설 스킨으로 압박감 속에서도 목적 의식을 잃지 않고 더 나은 미래를 위해 함께 싸우는 요르하의 대표 안드로이드들을 전장에 불러 보세요. 모두의 영광을 위해 싸우는 스킨 목록은 다음과 같습니다.
  • 2B 키리코
  • 9S 우양
  • A2 벤데타
  • 사령관 화이트 메르시
  • 아담 라이프위버
스킨은 개별 묶음 상품이나 대형 묶음 상품의 구성으로 판매됩니다. 콜라보 테마 로비로 출격 전의 분위기를 한껏 느껴 보세요. 이벤트 도전 과제를 완료하면 프로필 카드, 플레이어 칭호, 특별 무기 장식품 등 다양한 꾸미기 아이템을 획득할 수 있으니, 모두 콜라보레이션 이벤트 허브에서 확인해 보세요.

밝은 미래는 주어지는 것이 아니라, 싸워서 쟁취해야 합니다. 오버워치 x 프로젝트 요르하 콜라보레이션과 오늘 함께하세요!
지금 출격하기
방송 시작! 메이로 채팅창을 얼려 버리세요
신화 방송 시작! 메이로 화려하고 빛나는 매력을 뽐내며 스포트라이트를 한껏 받는 스트리머 판타지에 빠져들어 보세요. 그렇다고 귀여운 토끼에 너무 헤벌쭉하진 마세요. 큰코다칠 수 있거든요! 전투는 더 귀엽게, 처치는 더 차갑게, 그리고 구독과 좋아요 꼭 눌러 주세요!

신화 스킨으로 방송을 시작한 다음, 추가 헤어스타일, 헤드폰 스타일, 장밋빛 핑크와 강렬한 퍼플 펑크 배색, 픽셀 기반 공격을 강화해 주는 기술 시각 효과 전환, 빛나는 에너지로 분위기를 끌어올리는 주변 스프라이트 효과를 활용해 메이를 궁극의 아이돌로 완성시키세요.
방송 시작하기
멋지게 진영을 선택하세요
1시즌 프리미엄 배틀 패스로 오버워치의 새로운 제국과 시대 속 나만의 자리를 반영한 보상을 얻으세요. 진영을 테마로 한 꾸미기 아이템, 다섯 개의 전설 스킨, 500 크레딧 등 의미 있는 보상이 단계마다 기다립니다. 추가로 신화 프리즘을 최대 80개 획득해서 신화 메르시 천상의 수호자, 신화 방송 시작! 메이, 또는 주노의 스타 슈터 무기를 얻으세요!

궁극의 배틀 패스를 확보하고 프리미엄 트랙의 모든 콘텐츠와 20단계 건너뛰기, 2,000 오버워치 코인, 새로운 시대 소전과 새로운 제국 엠레 전설 스킨도 손에 넣어 보세요.
준비 완료
새로운 제트팩 캣 스킨으로 날아오르세요
1시즌 스타터 팩에는 프리미엄 배틀 패스나 새로운 상점 스킨을 구매하기에 충분한 1,000 오버워치 코인이 포함되어 있으며, 전투기 조종사 제트팩 캣 특급 스킨도 귀여운 보너스로 제공됩니다. 지금 오버워치 상점으로 달려가 이륙을 준비하세요!
지금 구매하기
소셜 서비스
인재 채용 | 회사 소개 | 개인정보 처리방침 | 법률 관련 | 고객지원
오버워치 관련 최신 소식, 특별 할인, 베타 및 기타 사항 안내 이메일에 관심이 없다면 수신 거부로 설정해 주시기 바랍니다. 모든 이메일을 수신하고 싶지 않다면 구독 해지를 클릭하세요. 전반적인 환경 설정은 이곳을 클릭하여 조정할 수 있습니다.
제한 사항이 적용됩니다. 모든 제품에 배틀코인을 사용한 결제가 가능한 것은 아닙니다. 배틀코인을 사용한 결제 화폐가 제품의 화폐와 일치해야 합니다. 현재 남아 있는 배틀코인을 확인하려면 account.battle.net을 방문하세요

Title: Building human skills on your team
Body: First-time CEOs, “AI washing,” and the iPod comeback. - March 10, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/fckoHuM9ey2035_cCr2HCA~~/AAQRxRA~/qKmTzjrxM-gvnS9s8sADGGtwbL8ZVkddXrduCst95xC9AJRb96oO0srQblUNPCbZ6eFbp7__3S5DPKXIfS9I8iq6CNfc-I7YXuO_p7DmUwZsrU6-68wmKqz6o3JBalReatfd5M8srzPqfOMGUinqcRtHWXR-W62cGfKFXRMjl1ABRetSJLlhrvyB-La-iRZexQMWS6CcLrdFwItILH4WhpPjhj6avH3Sk6yXhpTrzEw~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * Building human skills that matter most * Question of the week * Just for laughs * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/OFl0XyKC42w-UYgvwxHeRg~~/AAQRxRA~/qKmTzjrxM-gvnS9s8sADGGtwbL8ZVkddXrduCst95xC9AJRb96oO0srQblUNPCbZ6eFbp7__3S5DPKXIfS9I8iq6CNfc-I7YXuO_p7DmUwZsrU6-68wmKqz6o3JBalReatfd5M8srzPqfOMGUinqcbcKWtZhUuhvh4bqtUUficdnAkliLGDYVraaNlrkepGOdkP_Px6BD4vM35NE9Is1-YlTCJ1vTd3sZVo9gOoRL4c~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/dov4N9Wdvr-tVInvPxSREw~~/AAQRxRA~/kF8_4qrJ7phJeBy8Aq96Ci8dB0meho40AIV0PeDA2YOLqn5z3jzWoM2GtOArSve8B4SmRPtr8irxyiGR0-Ef5P00J5MTUrhjxwKXWv4kIDnDUap19DWUvMIgtaUkYj9Br_ffkgcXfMg74yaDv_gKV8OxEDYqgBZBFj2pjO92xtU~ ) Workplace trends Leadership First-time CEOs are taking over the corner office 168 new CEO appointments were made across the S&P 1500, the highest since 2010. 84% of those new appointments are stepping into the role for the very first time, according to Spencer Stuart's 2025 CEO Transitions Report. Rather than recruiting outside stars, boards are overwhelmingly promoting from within, with COOs and presidents accounting for nearly half of all new appointments. The underlying driver of these first-time appointments is compelling: Spencer Stuart's research finds that these ‘fresh’ CEOs, given the runway, tend to outperform more seasoned counterparts over the long haul, and in 2026, boardrooms appear ready to find out if that holds true. Skills Excel just became a competitive sport - and it's getting students hired Every year, students from dozens of universities compete in the Microsoft Excel World Championship, racing against the clock to solve complex spreadsheet challenges. The Microsoft-backed competition culminates in Las Vegas, broadcast on ESPN with a $100,000 prize fund. Last year's student winners outlasted 8,000 students from more than 70 schools worldwide - and several walked straight into job offers as a result, according to Fortune. Finance professors who advise these teams say the appeal goes beyond technical skill — as AI makes most resumes look similar, having something niche and demonstrable gives candidates a story to tell. The AI corner Labor Companies are blaming AI for layoffs it didn't cause A provocative term is gaining traction in business circles: "AI washing". OpenAI CEO Sam Altman acknowledged the phenomenon, saying some companies are attributing cuts to AI that they would have made regardless. The numbers support the skepticism, with nearly 90% of executives surveyed across four countries admitting that AI had no impact on employment since ChatGPT launched, according to the National Bureau of Economic Research. Yale Budget Lab researchers similarly found no significant AI-related labor shifts through late 2025, with the executive director telling Fortune the macroeconomic effects simply aren't showing up yet. Researchers suggest the more likely explanation is that companies are using AI as cover for cuts driven by margin pressure and geopolitical headwinds. Spending The AI buying frenzy is officially cooling down After a year of hype and wild spending, companies are getting a lot more careful about what AI tools they actually buy. AI software sales cycles that once closed in 60 to 90 days have stretched to six months, with legal and finance teams now showing up to procurement meetings that used to be left to tech leaders alone, according to the Wall Street Journal. Even in customer service — one of the most established areas for AI — only 11% of leaders say the technology met their primary business objective, per a Gartner survey. As the ROI on these tools comes into question, companies are demanding proof of real outcomes before opening their wallets. Building human skills that matter most By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/iD6jwvd1to1p8MoZggmMNA~~/AAQRxRA~/qKmTzjrxM-gvnS9s8sADGGtwbL8ZVkddXrduCst95xC9AJRb96oO0srQblUNPCbZ6eFbp7__3S5DPKXIfS9I8iq6CNfc-I7YXuO_p7DmUwZsrU6-68wmKqz6o3JBalReatfd5M8srzPqfOMGUinqcRqjoTcTDV3pIQ6CPckvr0ZsLrydBvK2TJmP_3DCcz0mSWvJyNm4umEeqpMCiIkynQy53L6S_1wjHgF8LRTQPSQ~ ) The more AI handles task-based work, the more visible and valuable human abilities have become. A recent HR Dive survey of more than 1,000 hiring managers found that communication topped the list of most desirable soft skills for employees, followed by professionalism, time management, accountability, resilience, and problem-solving. New AI technologies have certainly accelerated work execution, but humans are still the ones leading projects, influencing decisions, navigating ambiguity, and collaborating across teams. They’re also the ones building trust with clients, reading the room in meetings, and making judgment calls when the data isn’t clear. These competencies are uniquely human — and as a leader, it’s critical to help your team develop them. So how can you grow your employees’ soft skills? Proactively teach communication When projects move fast and have many moving parts, communication can easily break down. People assume everyone saw the same message, attended the same meeting, or knows how to use collaboration tools properly. But strong communication isn’t about saying something once; it’s about making sure it lands and creating shared visibility so fewer decisions happen in silos. Think about building a communications checklist with your team that includes every step needed to share important updates. This could mean discussing them in a meeting, followed by a written email recap, a post in a dedicated channel, and reinforcement through a calendar reminder. Creating these habits helps employees think differently about communication by not assuming everyone has heard and understood the message. Over time, this practice helps them build stronger communication skills in every part of their work. Key question: “How can you build consistent communication habits into your team’s workflow?” Define and celebrate professionalism On the surface, professionalism can look different across company cultures. Some teams wear hoodies, others wear suits. Some operate with rigid processes, while others thrive in flexibility. And because professionalism isn’t always defined explicitly, it can be helpful to discuss what it means for your team. Host a conversation about how employees can show up professionally for one another. That might mean arriving prepared, being proactive about problem-solving, respecting deadlines, and doing research before meetings. Whatever your team decides, write these behaviors down as a shared agreement between team members and take a couple of minutes to reinforce them in your all-hands meetings. Most importantly, celebrate professional behavior when you see it in action, giving your team strong examples of how to conduct themselves. These actions can build small habits that eventually build more professional employees. Key question: “What behaviors define professionalism on your team, and are you reinforcing them consistently?” Link time management to accountability Time management isn’t just about working faster; it’s about knowing where your time creates the most impact. If someone spends six hours on a low-impact task, it may not be a productivity issue – it could be a prioritization gap, a learning curve, or simply a lack of clarity about what matters most. With this in mind, consider using your one-on-ones to help employees connect the value of tasks to the time invested. When people understand impact, they make better decisions about effort, and clarity around priorities reduces overwhelm. If your employee is new to time management or struggling with it, you might help them create a simple weekly prioritization plan or set expectations for how long certain tasks should take, as a reference. It’s also important to encourage employees to let you know when something is taking longer than expected so you can troubleshoot together. Over time, your employees will find a rhythm of time management that works for them, while producing the desired outcomes. Key question: “How can you help your team align time spent with business impact?” Practice problem-solving AI is a powerful thought partner, but critical thinking remains a human advantage. In your team meetings, create moments where the group thinks through a challenge before turning to a tool. For example, you could present a real project obstacle in a meeting and ask: What are three possible solutions? What risks do we see? What assumptions are we making? Then invite AI into the conversation as a point of comparison. Then discuss: Where did it expand thinking? Where did human insight add nuance? By practicing structured problem-solving, you build confidence and resilience, which are two traits AI can’t automate. The more your team practices navigating ambiguity, the less intimidating complex decisions become. Key question: “Where can you encourage your team to think critically before defaulting to AI?” Water cooler chatter The iPod is making a comeback, and Gen Z is to thank. The appeal is simple: music with no distractions. The trend even has a name - "friction-maxxing" - choosing the inconvenience of loading songs one by one over letting Spotify do the thinking. "A smartphone throws music, messages, social feeds and news into one device, making it nearly impossible to keep your use in check." Cal Newport, Computer Science Professor and author of Digital Minimalism Buying a home with your best friend is becoming more mainstream. 15% of Americans have already purchased a home with a friend or relative, and younger generations are especially open to it, according to a recent FirstHome IQ survey. Experts say the key is putting the arrangement in writing upfront, before the friendship gets complicated. "Home values have gone up so much that people are getting creative just to get a foot in the door." Amanda Pendleton, Home Trends Expert, Zillow Question of the week Last week’s answer: 40% This week’s question: What percentage of senior leadership roles across Britain's biggest companies are held by women? Just for laughs Image https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/UVd10EvN-TyboPH5EMxmaw~~/AAQRxRA~/XljDGzEE5LuCm6SPEVFt1Y1PaDDNF-B8XCmBqe-Vjnzzdw1omW4UfiCHN5tQvB2oZxArn9FgvH4PXRkAXxNtMrmRtMbPA7wxhysWg-ifW35NuaTbmPMrdUxYeNv5kr96MZ5Wwd2674kASY2bjjNvsH8vzKuSuLfOBMnk-ob5IERquqpVRF2TiA9S_ZTAK1TvAQBw42YLcfrzYZ1j3JLgt4HQmjK8E6jZivJYLW0oCC8P4rzJyLOzj57lsaJyYCH0egzy4U_4pjcwd_zPv5nrsGn4p0xy8JoQO-SIFY0D19HkJJa9BNDS-iCCgHEnMv1DwPAnF0IA_YGhzKba3seGJNdVxJyH4emUKYgsLfcn5TMcomMF_7kypQe8fXNaP_8xq1uu8ov4e5T8fataJg-41DFQ3SsB2T3a_08DnxHO5IJzODldYA76yqR_EEP_YeYRP3spseNEpaS7U6OtLELwmCYFi0zqfErXpqMaSdzvPgl5bULRgxESeMKLVG8pI8Jt To update your email notification settings, click here ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/89PGwoWqTu0l359mTblVtw~~/AAQRxRA~/rB6AV22VfDpp0btw7wUw-fvaeM74q1EDO7yUvNlR1qb7dsZ7YNjiOVc_PmpmMt4sc93EGEvHLXE4iwzTeh3rc8J-YlZPyZ0fVy4z2lJ6OwWOGkChB9Mhpcb_3DGDpjAOfxQkJJp0THpAuFSheu5IGXCfOTJSMmu4yHnIc4oSH30~ ) . Title: [삼성증권] 안훈규 고객님의 자산 운용현황을 확인하실 수 있습니다
Body: 포트폴리오 Review 2026.02 포트폴리오 Review 고객님 안녕하십니까? 삼성증권입니다. 전월 투자성과와 포트폴리오 분석, 실현손익/잔고현황을 보내드립니다. 고객님의 향후 투자계획에 참고하여 주시기 바랍니다. 보다 자세한 내용이나 궁금하신 점은 고객님의 담당PB 또는 거래지점과 상담하시기 바랍니다. 감사합니다. 포트폴리오 Review 첨부파일 확인 방법 첨부파일 클릭 생년월일 입력 보고서 확인 포트폴리오 Review 란? 매월 초 전월의 투자성과 및 포트폴리오 분석을 간략하게 요약해 제공해 드리는 사후관리 서비스입니다. 일부 메일계정에서는 첨부파일 바로 열기가 지원되지 않을 수 있습니다. 이러한 경우 첨부를 저장한 후 실행하십시오. 회사에서 사용하시는 PC의 경우 회사 내 보안 정책으로 인하여 보안메일이 열리지 않을 수 있습니다. 본 메일은 발신전용으로 회신이 안되오니 문의사항이 있으시면 삼성증권 Family Center(1588-2323)로 연락하시기 바랍니다. 메일 수신을 원하지 않으시면 수신거부 를 클릭하여 주시기 바랍니다. ▶고객정보변경 06620 서울특별시 서초구 서초대로74길 11 삼성전자빌딩 | 삼성증권주식회사 | 대표이사 박종문 | 사업자등록번호:202-81-48588 COPYRIGHTⓒ SAMSUNG SECURITIES, ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

( https://link.coursera.org/f/a/aol_F4GI6KqfNeu1WyexTg~~/AAQRxRA~/lML9pcF2D_yO3RqY5AO13ffkhaymYN8noT1yroS9e6d2R5rNlbAw14HaeiDQBvdLvq5WzF1ShgPLqkM-dnWWNREDc3gW1C5J-rRnwNPU2QsIjm6lR3prjPwVY6_m-NwEqcwGTZIh8MC5713h3jxh6X3lXYHgrxKZXgs6nxwyDCI~?lid=aube3idoqszp ) Google AI Professional Certificate ( https://link.coursera.org/f/a/ADj55glwcnaarF2PGsqliA~~/AAQRxRA~/mjMBCnUCHT5XC52EpABy4Kq8KzrsqfFRzLlEqUtozLbNVokHejz1HWADzj44aVp7hygNkD38Mtuf41j5UxyANRvtxLjMN4Q_LQaSykWUUYsSModnIFo-DYhKyTm8EqD9hInP3y08GmxVOzZU8jBXGyrJU4_dW2gnlIorgwx8j3JYE01ZxHGmP0eF2TRWwCOs ) ************************************************** Develop AI fluency in 10 hours with Google experts ************************************************** Experience matters, but AI skills are taking the lead—70% of employers prioritize candidates with AI skills over one with more years of experience.1 With the new Google AI Professional Certificate ( https://link.coursera.org/f/a/TXf7M55E6T00RvR-IHF5_Q~~/AAQRxRA~/mjMBCnUCHT5XC52EpABy4Kq8KzrsqfFRzLlEqUtozLbNVokHejz1HWADzj44aVp7hygNkD38Mtuf41j5UxyANeDgQv9FBUabb7XD7Qno8xekPCbI-L_9yvuEegbJVZEhmJiaYWxEeyLkaoQUkUKrp3vT9DAljfpBUtOTLCGcmRyLSbWGS74LK2E4B1MYW65M ) , you can build the in-demand AI skills they’re looking for. In just 10 hours, you can learn to: 👉 Learn to effectively prompt AI to get the results you actually want 👉 Analyze data and tailor content in a fraction of the time 👉 Vibe code practical apps for your daily work using plain language Enroll today to earn a trusted credential and get three months of Google AI Pro at no cost. Get the skills, the portfolio, and the certificate to show you’re ready for the AI-powered workforce. Title: (광고) SouthernCros 님, 오버워치 대 탈론 이벤트에서 미래를 위해 싸우고 보상을 획득하세요!
Body: 진영을 선택하고, 최대 75개의 전리품 상자와 12개의 특급 전리품 상자, 7개의 전설 전리품 상자를 획득하세요! 18175 진영 선택 ( https://links.blizzard.com/f/a/ATQz7e2kjmGYorHcf_T02g~~/AAQRxRA~/U71guPIpkE6r2rPdV2HnUNFjBNvQ3gf0lABIwYlUG7PWupRop1DOgYxUbKaII2DH06WhXX9Kirpd3YJZcAY_ziDqNbkFrr_m86FMJ64MmUuhWZwsjQJIbVl7SIV1K8H_mOyRfbHKiSQtO0qeFMELKQRGPVXNQh4ihy2zNjdrv36QMdyreBRrvxluig9YjCBODvifjX_3S0BxqyKpqoBHD_EzCf_Py6Dz8FC_M0YV79b9AgmKcs_nQNtOglis8Dh2xmfmw6N2Giao3vpX49TNE88G_P1eiHMZTZnZMOpJPIZKpPXVr4GxQ49qwTzO7Jq- ) 진영 선택 오버워치가 용맹하게 돌격하고 있습니다. SouthernCros 님이 아는 세상을 지키시겠습니까, 아니면 정면으로 맞서겠습니까? 전세가 흔들리고 있습니다. 진영을 선택하고 지금 이 싸움에 흔적을 남기세요. 더 나은 내일을 위해 싸우든, 힘으로 세상을 다시 빚어내든, 아래 보상을 획득할 수 있습니다. * 기본 전리품 상자 75개 * 특급 전리품 상자 12개 * 전설 전리품 상자 7개 * 신규 음성 대사 9개 * 2D 꾸미기 아이템 31개 * 무기 장식품 2개 미래는 앞으로 어떻게 하느냐에 달려 있습니다. 싸움을 멈추지 말고 결말을 확인하세요. 계속 싸우기 ( Title: Using AI to ease change
Body: Clarify priorities, reduce busywork, simplify decisions. - March 3, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/QjMfdPi_C4ImxcEymkrkag~~/AAQRxRA~/WOa7LCEHRdgniiajDidb8sVb9nkeqJGPWPhGVxOJ0U9udpOcU_aJS1ecqan_Yj9zV-Bz5YCvjmCoHat-cI_Jz3WbnywHC0HIlzWpL1TU6dlWDyPjAr6H-BTnmShK9S7cMT5-7f0NlxWYu6H9KJWMMxYo2aCcgOKhoeWWskxFKhbUgTsNqHfHfTXgbU2eF4vKpphvTwusArizK0AM7RgH_c92Sxpz53flJKdQ2F9-zT0~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * Using AI to ease change fatigue * Question of the week * Just for laughs * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/mqH8AM2FDel8dtt0yeO55g~~/AAQRxRA~/WOa7LCEHRdgniiajDidb8sVb9nkeqJGPWPhGVxOJ0U9udpOcU_aJS1ecqan_Yj9zV-Bz5YCvjmCoHat-cI_Jz3WbnywHC0HIlzWpL1TU6dlWDyPjAr6H-BTnmShK9S7cMT5-7f0NlxWYu6H9KJWMM--PEfXOaSbt15_f9_Sj_dgkTfRVEp7Dhl0IYWVX1m1Ra_x9u7RyRxoV3SmuyhYW0V-6lxCnuLaDw58NuYX-dFE~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/RjcR_y332w0qxafj8ZOloA~~/AAQRxRA~/hdpNSrTU4rGyEo0hzGFkHwGwWj1VozR7Ksk14jwoW3o-Y5bGKg_bXf5KtbSR_KVSkKDzb68Uxek9mx1FvqM2Yi5ZiKk7MeP1OPr-YWUAMWY28_DFcBoLFd3vy1qisv-FwGxr8h4geStT-exOc4WPAQWByJ4pLVYJJYRyzA4_gqU~ ) Workplace trends Education Gen Z performs lower than previous generations, thanks to increased screentime Gen Z is now the first generation in modern history to score lower on standardized tests than the previous one, neuroscientist Jared Cooney Horvath told the US Senate earlier this year. Ironically, while the US spent more than $30 billion to put laptops and tablets in schools in 2024, the data shows a stark correlation between screen time in school and worse test scores. Students engage in off-task activities on their computers nearly two-thirds of the time, and when attention is interrupted, it weakens memory formation and increases errors. These declining cognitive capabilities could have lasting implications for Gen Z's workplace performance and career prospects. Environment India's pollution crisis is becoming a business problem This past winter brought a stark reminder of how widespread the impact of India’s pollution problem has become. Major retailers reported reduced consumer spending in Q4 2025, executives are turning down roles in polluted cities, and governments are issuing travel advisories, according to The Economist. The disruption spilled into public life too, with cricket matches called off due to smog and international athletes withdrawing from competitions in Delhi. Economists note that unlike inflation, air pollution's economic damage tends to be invisible enough to avoid urgent policy responses. The AI corner Healthcare China is using AI to modernize traditional acupuncture A brainwave-controlled glove that performs acupuncture for stroke recovery patients is among the latest AI-powered gadgets developed by Chinese companies to modernize traditional medicine. The Chinese government released a five-year plan calling for AI to discover new treatments and sensors that detect pulse rhythms for health diagnoses, according to The Economist. AI chatbots are helping doctors choose treatment concoctions, and applications offer dietary advice based on photos of users' tongues. One software engineer building AI models for traditional medicine clinics says AI-powered diagnoses have reached the level of an "average" doctor in the field. Productivity AI tools promise to liberate workers from email overload The average employee receives 117 emails and 153 Teams messages daily, with interruptions happening every two minutes during core work hours, according to a Microsoft study. AI-powered email assistants are flooding the market to tackle this problem, with at least 85 companies attracting $581 million in funding. Google rolled out its Gemini AI to all Gmail users in January, while Microsoft integrated Copilot with Outlook to help prioritize inboxes. The tools promise to reclaim hours now lost to inbox management and free workers from time-consuming administrative tasks. Using AI to ease change fatigue By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/gZz2EHSFLcOgJwOPOuTwGw~~/AAQRxRA~/WOa7LCEHRdgniiajDidb8sVb9nkeqJGPWPhGVxOJ0U9udpOcU_aJS1ecqan_Yj9zV-Bz5YCvjmCoHat-cI_Jz3WbnywHC0HIlzWpL1TU6dlWDyPjAr6H-BTnmShK9S7cMT5-7f0NlxWYu6H9KJWMMydo06tyVXRPIOvTT-8FCjf8darWTcjOrRnLBCW4KEFZmVVJtlw8uJpkOzlhTVR5SDEDEwfQcJN2XNwghSAXV9I~ ) Digital transformation isn’t new, but AI has dramatically sped it up. Teams aren’t just learning new tools and workflows; they’re constantly adjusting strategies and priorities while expectations continue to climb. But ironically, the biggest driver of organizational change is also the most powerful tool for rising to the challenge. According to Forbes, workers across departments have reported using AI to complete tasks between 14-55% faster, depending on the task. As a leader, your job is to help your team think beyond using AI for content creation and basic automation and empower them to use it to adapt to constant change. When you can help them think bigger about AI, they can adapt to change faster, reduce their cognitive load, and feel empowered to drive those new strategies. Remember, the goal isn’t just to help people survive constant change, but to give them tools that make change feel more manageable and even motivating. When employees see AI reducing friction in their daily work by helping them prioritize, analyze information faster, and make better decisions, change starts to feel less like disruption and more like progress. So how can you use AI to help your team stay energized through constant change? Clarify priorities quickly When priorities shift, employees spend mental energy trying to figure out what matters most, which heavily contributes to their change fatigue. The best way you can help is by providing clarity, but sometimes you’re still working through the change yourself. So, when a shift happens, try feeding your previous strategy, your team’s current priorities, and the new direction into your AI platform. Ask it to help you translate the change into updated priorities for your team, then refine the output so it reflects your team’s reality and expectations. This process helps you think through what needs to happen so you can communicate a clearer plan. You might also encourage team members to use AI as a thought partner to translate their new objectives into updated daily tasks, helping them adjust faster and with less guesswork. Key question: “How can you use AI to help define and communicate new top priorities faster after a change?” Reduce busywork Every change creates extra work, such as updating documentation, revising plans, creating new presentations, or reworking messaging. These transition tasks often pile on top of normal responsibilities and quickly drain mental energy. As part of your planning, map out the new deliverables for the updated strategy, then distribute them across your team. Identify where your team can use AI to speed up work, then have an open conversation on when to use it and where human checkpoints are needed. For example, an employee might use AI to update competitive battlecards based on new messaging, but they should still review and refine the content before sharing it. When AI handles the first iteration of a project and employees focus on judgment and accuracy, the workload feels far more manageable. Key question: “What transition tasks could AI handle so your team can focus on meaningful work?” Simplify everyday decisions Constant change brings a steady stream of small decisions, like what to tackle first, which requests are most urgent, or where to focus limited time. AI can help by organizing information and suggesting next steps so employees don’t have to sort through everything on their own. Encourage your team to use AI to summarize long threads, highlight key takeaways from meetings, or turn large project updates into simple action lists. Maybe this means using AI to turn a weekly status update into three clear priorities or comparing options by outlining pros and cons before making a decision. When AI helps narrow the choices, employees spend less energy deciding what to do and more energy doing the actual work, making it easier to stay resilient as change continues. Key question: “Where could AI help simplify everyday decisions for your team?” Build simple AI habits Many teams experiment with AI during times of change, but never turn those experiments into everyday routines. When that happens, each new shift feels like starting from scratch. Instead of only using AI at pivotal moments, help your team build a few simple habits they can rely on regularly. You might even set aside a few minutes in team meetings for people to share prompts or shortcuts that have saved them time. When AI becomes part of the normal workflow, it stops feeling like one more thing to learn and starts becoming a steady support system. Having consistent habits makes it easier for your team to adapt because they already have tried and true tools and workflows. Key question: “How can you help your team build simple AI habits that support them during change?” Water cooler chatter Your Zoom glitch may just cost you the job. Even minor video disruptions - a freeze, an echo, a blank screen - significantly reduce the chances of a candidate being hired, according to new research from Cornell and Columbia. "Although the internet is meant to be an equalizer, unreliable connectivity can instead hold people back." Jacqueline Rifkin, Assistant Professor of Marketing, Cornell University Gen Z is buying devices to block their phones. Products like Bloom and Brick are stainless steel cards that users tap to their phones to lock themselves out of addictive apps, according to Fortune. Bloom has sold more than 60,000 units since launching in 2024. The devices create a physical barrier that makes doomscrolling a conscious decision rather than an automatic habit. "In the 1900s, everyone was smoking cigarettes, and it was just normal, until the studies came out that it's bad for you. I think it's very similar for phone use." Giancarlo Novelli, Cofounder of Bloom and UCLA senior Question of the week Last week’s answer: 38% This week’s question: What percent of Stanford undergraduates receive disability accommodations? Title: 알리익스프레스 문의에 대한 후속 조치 / 1119306153813814
Body: 안녕하십니까 고객님. 알리 익스프레스를 찾아주셔서 감사합니다. [ 1119306153813814 ] 문의 주신 내용에 대해 답변을 드립니다. 해당 주문 건으로 불편 드려 죄송합니다. 해당 주문건은 두개의 패키지로 배송되는 상품으로 확인됩니다. 해당 상품에 대해 당사에서 반드시 도착보장 약속하는 시일이 [ 2026.04.02 ]인 점 안내드립니다. 조금만 더 기다려주시기 바랍니다. 환불 원하시나 현재 환불 신청이 어려우신 상황일 경우, 해당 일자까지 배송이 도착하지 않았을 시 분쟁을 신청하시어 환불 진행 가능합니다. 분쟁 신청하는 경로 안내드립니다. PC 사용시 우측상단 계정 > 내 주문 > 주문상세 > 환불/반품 APP 사용시 우측하단 계정 > 주문 전체보기 > 주문상세 > 환불/반품 에서 진행 가능합니다. 신청시 "패키지를 받으셨습니까?" 에서 "아니오"를 누르고 진행하셔야 배송을 받기 전의 상황에 대해 분쟁 접수가 가능하시니 이 부분 참고부탁드립니다. 상황에 맞게 알맞은 카테고리로 진행해주셔야 원활한 분쟁진행이 가능한 점 양해부탁드립니다. 양해와 협조에 감사드리며 불편들 끼쳐드린 점 다시 한번 사과드립니다. 즐거운 하루 되십시오! AliExpress 고객 서비스팀 http://www.aliexpress.com Thank you for your consultation. Your current Service No. is:#CaseNo:2053807414466266#

Title: 유비소프트에 오신 걸 환영합니다
Body: 유비소프트에 오신 걸 환영합니다 ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ 유비소프트의 세계가 기다립니다! | https://view.updates.ubisoft.com/?%3D 브라우저에서 보기 https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? 계속 소식을 받아보고 싶다면? 지금 바로 가입하고 맞춤형 소식과 이벤트 정보, 커뮤니티 콘텐츠 및 특별 혜택*도 받아 보세요. https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? 소식 받기 https://click.updates.ubisoft.com/? Title: Ubisoft에 오신 걸 환영합니다
Body: 님 안녕하세요, Ubisoft에 오신 것을 환영합니다. 계정 정보 및 비밀번호를 설정하려면 여기 (https://account.ubisoft.com/ko-KR/action/set-password?locale=en&strLogin=guest-6gbZEEZ3&strEmail=dksgnsrb@gmail.com&strStamp=66F779863AA71A10417E38A248A0E5A989BDE76ABD5EF4AD56AA2E283F0718E714D9A3D6CF9300721A2AE63C208A876C36D7EFA5EA5267F9349D442536DC9D4F&genomeid=8a7cd23e-0efc-497f-8d61-5a9447029efe)를 클릭해 주세요. 이제 다음의 다양한 서비스를 이용할 수 있습니다: • 게임의 온라인 서비스 • Ubisoft Connect (https://ubisoftconnect.com)는 모든 플랫폼에서 보상, 통계, 팁을 비롯한 플레이어 서비스를 제공하는 시스템입니다 • Ubisoft Connect PC는 PC 플레이어에게만 서비스되는 자사의 데스크톱 앱입니다 • 상점 (https://store.ubisoft.com)의 할인과 프로모션 • Ubisoft 포럼과 커뮤니티 사이트 • Ubisoft 고객 지원 광고 뉴스레터를 통해 구독 설정을 변경할 수 있으며, 계정 관리 웹사이트의 "개인 정보 센터" (https://account.ubisoft.com/ko-KR/communications) 섹션에서 언제든 개인정보 및 광고 설정을 관리할 수 있습니다. 감사합니다. Ubisoft 팀

Title: A better Medium feed is a few clicks away
Body: Take a moment to refine your recommendations. Medium. ( https://medium.com?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--reco ) Make Medium truly yours. ( https://medium.com/me/following/suggestions?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--reco-------------------------a3e80e88_1340_11f1_8f20_4e6c74b472bb ) Hi Dksgnsrb, Are you seeing the stories that matter most to you? Your Medium feed relies on the strength of your recommendations—and your recommendations are shaped by the writers, publications, and topics you follow. Are you ready for a feed refresh? Take a moment to refine your recommendations ( https://medium.com/me/following/suggestions?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--reco-------------------------a3e80e88_1340_11f1_8f20_4e6c74b472bb ). It’ll help you discover some new writers, find relevant publications, and improve your reading experience on Medium. Title: [모니모] 모니모 이용약관 개정 안내
Body:
모니모 이용약관 개정 안내
고객님, 안녕하세요.
모니모를 이용해 주셔서 감사합니다.
고객님께 더 편리한 서비스를 제공하기 위해 모니모 이용약관이 일부 개정됩니다.
개정된 약관은 2026.4.30(목)부터 시행될 예정입니다.
아래 내용을 참고해 주세요.
감사합니다.
시행일
2026.4.30(목)
주요 변경 내용
① 제3조 (약관의 명시·교부·설명) 휴대폰 메시지 정의 기재
② 제4조 (약관의 효력 및 변경) 고지 채널 추가
③ 제18조 (정보의 제공) 고지 채널 추가
④ 제19조 (서비스 이용의 일시 중단) 고지 채널 추가
⑤ 제20조 (서비스 이용 정지 및 해지) 고지 채널 추가, 서비스 이용 정지 및 해지 항목 구체화
⑥ 제30조 (관할법원) 관련 법률명 추가
구분 변경 전 변경 후
제3조 (약관의 명시·교부·설명) ① 회사는 회원이 약관의 내용을 쉽게 알 수 있도록 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게시하며, 회원이 요구할 경우 이메일, 팩스 전송, 우편, 직접 교부, 휴대폰메시지(SMS, LMS) 중 1가지 방법으로 약관의 사본을 회원에게 교부합니다. ① 회사는 회원이 약관의 내용을 쉽게 알 수 있도록 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게시하며, 회원이 요구할 경우 이메일, 팩스 전송, 우편, 직접 교부, 휴대폰 메시지(휴대폰 메시지란 휴대폰 문자메시지(SMS 등)와 모바일 메시징 서비스를 통한 모바일 메시지를 의미합니다. 모바일 메시지의 경우 회원의 사전 동의를 받아야 하며, 데이터 비용 발생사실 등을 안내하여야 합니다. 또한, 유효하게 전달되지 못한 경우 휴대폰 문자메시지(SMS 등)로 대체전송됩니다. 이하 같습니다.) 중 1가지 방법으로 약관의 사본을 회원에게 교부합니다.
제4조 (약관의 효력 및 변경) ② 회사가 이 약관을 변경하고자 하는 경우에는 변경사유 및 적용일자, 변경내용을 명시하여 변경 1개월 전에 그 내용을 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게시(기존 회원에 대한 변경약관의 적용 여부, 신∙구대비표 포함)하고, 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰메시지(SMS, LMS) 등1가지 이상의 방법으로 통지(기존 회원에 대한 변경약관의 적용 여부, 신∙구대비표 포함)합니다. 단, 회원이 이의를 제기할 경우 회사는 회원에게 적절한 방법으로 약관 변경내용을 통지하였음을 확인해 드립니다. ② 회사가 이 약관을 변경하고자 하는 경우에는 변경사유 및 적용일자, 변경내용을 명시하여 변경 1개월 전에 그 내용을 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게시(기존 회원에 대한 변경약관의 적용 여부, 신∙구대비표 포함)하고, 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰 메시지 등 회원이 사전에 동의(선택)한 수단 중 1가지 이상의 방법으로 통지(기존 회원에 대한 변경약관의 적용 여부, 신∙구대비표 포함)합니다. 단, 회원이 이의를 제기할 경우 회사는 회원에게 적절한 방법으로 약관 변경내용을 통지하였음을 확인해 드립니다.
제18조 (정보의 제공) ① 회사는 서비스의 운영과 관련한 공지사항을 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게재하거나 휴대폰메시지(SMS, LMS) 등을 통하여 통지할 수 있습니다.
② 회사는 서비스 운영과 관련한 각종 정보를 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게재하는 등의 방법으로 이용회원에게 제공할 수 있으며, 중요 정보는 휴대폰메시지(SMS, LMS) 등을 통하여 개별적으로 통지합니다.
③ 회사는 회원에게 광고성 정보 및 회원 맞춤형 정보를 제공할 수 있으며, 해당 정보는 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게재하거나 회원이 사전에 동의한 경우에 한하여 휴대폰메시지(SMS, LMS), 앱 푸시 등의 전자적 방법을 통해 발송됩니다. 광고성 정보는 관련 법령에 따라 사전 수신 동의를 받는 경우에 한하여 제공됩니다.		 ① 회사는 서비스의 운영과 관련한 공지사항을 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게재하거나 휴대폰 메시지 등 회원이 사전에 동의(선택)한 수단을 통하여 통지할 수 있습니다.
② 회사는 서비스 운영과 관련한 각종 정보를 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게재하는 등의 방법으로 이용회원에게 제공할 수 있으며, 중요 정보는 휴대폰 메시지 등 회원이 사전에 동의(선택)한 수단을 통하여 개별적으로 통지합니다.
③ 회사는 회원에게 광고성 정보 및 회원 맞춤형 정보를 제공할 수 있으며, 해당 정보는 모니모 홈페이지 및 모니모 앱에 게재하거나 회원이 사전에 동의한 경우에 한하여 휴대폰 메시지, 앱 푸시 등의 전자적 방법을 통해 발송됩니다. 광고성 정보는 관련 법령에 따라 사전 수신 동의를 받는 경우에 한하여 제공됩니다.
제19조 (서비스 이용의 일시 중단) ② 회사는 전항의 사유로 서비스의 이용을 중단 또는 제한하고자 하는 경우에는 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰메시지(SMS, LMS) 등 1가지 이상의 방법을 통해 3영업일 이전에 그 사유와 내용을 사전에 회원에게 통지합니다. 단, 전산 장애 발생 등의 불가피한 사유로 긴급하게 이용을 중단하거나 제한할 필요가 있는 경우에는 사유 발생 즉시 공지 및 통지 합니다.
③ 회사는 서비스 이용의 일시 중단 상태가 해소되는 경우 사유발생 당일에 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰메시지(SMS, LMS) 등 1가지 이상의 방법을 통해 회원에게 알려드립니다.		 ② 회사는 전항의 사유로 서비스의 이용을 중단 또는 제한하고자 하는 경우에는 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰 메시지 등 회원이 사전에 동의(선택)한 수단 중 1가지 이상의 방법을 통해 3영업일 이전에 그 사유와 내용을 사전에 회원에게 통지합니다. 단, 전산 장애 발생 등의 불가피한 사유로 긴급하게 이용을 중단하거나 제한할 필요가 있는 경우에는 사유 발생 즉시 공지 및 통지 합니다.
③ 회사는 서비스 이용의 일시 중단 상태가 해소되는 경우 사유발생 당일에 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰 메시지 등 회원이 사전에 동의(선택)한 수단 중 1가지 이상의 방법을 통해 회원에게 알려드립니다.
제20조 (서비스 이용 정지 및 해지) ② 회사는 회원에게 다음 각 호의 어느 하나에 해당하는 사유가 있는 경우 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰메시지(SMS, LMS) 등 1가지 이상의 방법을 통해 사유 발생 당일 고지 후 서비스 이용 계약을 정지할 수 있습니다.
③ 회사는 회원의 서비스 이용 정지 상태가 해소되는 경우 사유 발생 당일에 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰메시지(SMS, LMS) 등 1가지 이상의 방법을 통해 회원에게 안내합니다.		 ② 회사는 회원에게 다음 각 호의 어느 하나에 해당하는 사유가 있는 경우 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰 메시지 등 회원이 사전에 동의(선택)한 수단 중 1가지 이상의 방법을 통해 사유 발생 당일 고지 후 서비스 이용 계약을 정지할 수 있습니다.
③ 회사는 회원의 서비스 이용 정지 상태가 해소되는 경우 사유 발생 당일에 서면, 이메일, 휴대폰 메시지 등 회원이 사전에 동의(선택)한 수단 중 1가지 이상의 방법을 통해 회원에게 안내합니다.
④ 회원이 다음 각 호의 어느 하나에 해당하는 사유가 있을 경우 10영업일 전에 서비스 이용계약이 해지될 수 있음을 알려드리며, 10영업일이 경과할 경우 서비스 이용계약을 해지할 수 있습니다.
6. 회사의 분할, 합병, 영업양도, 영업의 폐지 등 회사의 경영상 중대한 필요에 의한 경우
7. 서비스의 이용 목적을 벗어난 회원 개인의 영리목적 또는 부정한 방법으로 이용한 경우		 ④ 회원이 다음 각 호의 어느 하나에 해당하는 사유가 있을 경우 10영업일 전에 서비스 이용계약이 해지될 수 있음을 알려드리며, 10영업일이 경과할 경우 서비스 이용계약을 해지할 수 있습니다.
6. 회사의 휴·폐업, 영업폐지 등 회사의 경영상 중대한 문제가 발생하여 계약을 지속하기 어려운 경우
7. 기타 이 약관에 위배되거나 위법 또는 서비스의 이용 목적을 벗어난 회원 개인의 영리목적 이용 등 제1호 내지 제6호에 준하는 부정한 방법으로 이용한 경우
제30조(관할법원) 본 약관에 따른 거래에 관하여 분쟁이 발생하는 경우에 관할법원은 민사소송법에 따릅니다. 본 약관에 따른 거래에 관하여 분쟁이 발생하는 경우에 관할법원은 금융소비자보호법 및 민사소송법에 따릅니다.
꼭 확인하세요
• 이 약관 개정 안내 후 약관 시행일 전의 영업일까지 별도의 계약 해지 의사를 표시하지 않는 경우, 변경에 동의한 것으로 봅니다.

마일리지 적립 및 사용

아시아나클럽은 아시아나항공의 상용우대 프로그램으로 ‘아시아나 마일리지’를 기반으로 운영됩니다. 아시아나클럽 회원은 아시아나항공 및 제휴사를 이용하실 때마다 마일리지를 적립하실 수 있으며 적립된 마일리지로 마일리지 항공권 이용 등이 가능합니다.

우수회원 승급

아시아나항공 탑승 횟수, 아시아나항공 및 스타얼라이언스 항공편의 탑승마일리지, 제휴카드 적립 실적에 따라 우수회원으로 승급되면 더 많은 혜택을 누리실 수 있습니다. 신규 가입을 하면 최초 실버 등급(일반회원)이 되시며, 실적에 따라 골드, 다이아몬드, 다이아몬드 플러스, 플래티늄 회원으로 승급됩니다.

아시아나항공 뉴스레터

뉴스레터를 통해 , 마일리지 현황 및 아시아나항공의 다양한 상품정보 및 이벤트를 이메일로 받아 보실 수 있습니다.

마일리지 사용/적립몰

항공기 탑승 외에도 아시아나항공 홈페이지 내 ‘마일리지 적립몰‘ 및 ‘마일리지 사용몰‘ 이용 시 마일리지 적립, 사용이 가능합니다.

회원전용 이벤트 참여하기

아시아나항공 웹사이트에서 진행하는 다양한 이벤트에 참여하실 수 있습니다.

본 메일은 아시아나항공에 입력하신 이메일 주소로 발송되었으며, 발신 전용이므로 문의사항은 홈페이지 고객센터를 이용해 주시기 바랍니다.

Title: How to let go as a manager
Body: Corporate influencers ᐧ Goodbye resumes ᐧ AI financial advisers - February 24, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/DkUkr8RBPzst5Wde3a-2mg~~/AAQRxRA~/dhFANrvBq9Vjlmn1Y8f5t4XFNpON-ZF1G56h2OafCqcS6FFfwof5pk1HYsDOUuYVHSP2HfSKtCVBcSMdMWnErGnz4D8HjEkQ7NAd2tiVygMTakOEhRDSf9p0nS-ydFnom7OB4UX7EKeMs9018eiZmITUZijI3e4UYi7a98NSRZVu7NGlzJ0CKQLEtmbrCb5rfIvgzQ2nJTunvqrJ5-Teawngv3mG76RutOoOIgfIYSo~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * How to let go as a manager * Question of the week * Just for laughs * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/wnkaYHPBQqrZtW_Eu8GyGQ~~/AAQRxRA~/dhFANrvBq9Vjlmn1Y8f5t4XFNpON-ZF1G56h2OafCqcS6FFfwof5pk1HYsDOUuYVHSP2HfSKtCVBcSMdMWnErGnz4D8HjEkQ7NAd2tiVygMTakOEhRDSf9p0nS-ydFnom7OB4UX7EKeMs9018eiZmHcmJIOumMlxvYihsKuBltcr-CMr6SGvP4Lt5g7g8hTLsCeTlEuz_yV7gMv7QHdZbK99SL65YjbXYZcsX4RhZxk~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/cBahPSEWbRL3oDnKbrOBAw~~/AAQRxRA~/r4a1Qhqaj8H8SFZmCESKx7Y9yCUXbR5VSxc5exPXp5TKRBddPk3GGLJPnta9rF2xi71exOqEnbvMapmeFmfWxhtl_yhu_M7t7gq-59dn14b-ZaSIw4kFuQOsPOwkZwhCAAUgquw2vQVA9DjKZ-fUVffM4KU7Tda2pDj2el4xe2U~ ) Workplace trends Influencers Corporate influencers are turning office life into content gold Over 1 million TikTok videos are now tagged #9to5 or #CorporateTikTok, with creators building massive audiences by documenting their daily office routines, according to The Economist. "Corporate Natalie" has amassed more than 50 million likes by making fun of office culture - like people getting overly excited about receiving emails or using corporate buzzwords like "synergy" in everyday conversation. Companies have embraced the trend, with Deloitte hiring in-house influencers whose LinkedIn accounts are estimated to generate $13 million in annual advertising value. Hiring Résumés are losing their power in the hiring process Elon Musk recently told tech podcasters that hiring managers should trust face-to-face interactions over paper qualifications, explaining that after interviewing thousands of employees in SpaceX's early years, he learned a 20-minute conversation reveals more than any résumé. Hiring from prestigious companies based on pedigree alone can be misleading, as strong backgrounds don't always translate to actual performance, according to Fortune. Musk now tells his staff to look for evidence of exceptional ability rather than focusing on where candidates previously worked, a change that has helped Tesla's senior leadership maintain an average tenure of 10 to 12 years. The AI corner Finance AI chatbots make terrible financial advisers LLMs can't be trusted with your money, according to MIT finance professor Andrew Lo, who describes them as smooth and persuasive but completely devoid of financial understanding. The fundamental problem, he explains, is that today's AI delivers both good and bad financial advice with equally convincing confidence. This hasn't stopped adoption though - 19% of everyday investors across 13 countries now use ChatGPT-style tools to manage their portfolios, according to a survey commissioned by eToro. Researchers are working to develop specialized AI that could eventually serve as a true fiduciary adviser. Until then, financial professionals warn that relying on current AI for investment decisions could be particularly dangerous for inexperienced individual investors. Healthcare Technology helps workers with dementia stay employed longer Automatic scheduling systems, biometric authentication, and voice-activated assistants can help an aging population navigate cognitive challenges in their workplace, according to research from the University of Bath. Chatbots can transform complex documents into simplified formats instantly, while text-prediction language models help people with aphasia find words they struggle to articulate. As workers stay employed longer, implementing technology to lower cognitive loads will become essential, James Fletcher, assistant professor of digital futures, told The Wall Street Journal. How to let go as a manager By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/Z24lLZOkZ53w5LHeCbvpLA~~/AAQRxRA~/dhFANrvBq9Vjlmn1Y8f5t4XFNpON-ZF1G56h2OafCqcS6FFfwof5pk1HYsDOUuYVHSP2HfSKtCVBcSMdMWnErGnz4D8HjEkQ7NAd2tiVygMTakOEhRDSf9p0nS-ydFnom7OB4UX7EKeMs9018eiZmHUDCrr2h4JuIldikwls3qyPgg_Zc2F8R4ay9UuP7ANKOXfdqzquHPIrjrwATjAAiejQSzR0l0E6aG_igbOueLk~ ) In the early days of a team, managers wear dozens of hats and build almost everything themselves. But as a team grows and AI begins to take on more task-based work, leaders have to let go in order to move forward. And honestly, it’s not always easy. If you’ve traditionally done every job on your team, hiring people to take work off your plate can feel both exciting and unsettling. On one hand, you’re relieved to have help, but on the other, you might worry if the work will meet your standards. You may even feel disoriented without the comfort of the role you’ve always known, especially as your responsibilities shift into unfamiliar territory. Molly Graham, former Facebook and Google leader, famously calls this “giving away your Legos,” a metaphor for letting go of work so your team can help you build something bigger. Feeling apprehensive about delegation is normal, but letting go is critical. When you automate repetitive tasks with AI and hire specialists to take on work you once owned, you create space to focus on higher-level leadership, like setting direction, strategy, coaching your team, and anticipating what’s next. In other words, you and your team can do far more than you could by yourself. So how can you let go in a way to empower your team and elevate impact? Redefine what your value actually is One reason managers cling to responsibilities is that some tasks are tied to identity. Maybe you were promoted because you were the strongest writer, the most savvy developer, or the most detail-oriented project manager. Letting go of that work can feel like giving up the very thing that made you valuable. But leadership isn’t about being the best individual contributor in the room. It’s about creating the conditions for others to succeed. As AI absorbs more executional tasks, your impact shifts toward direction-setting, prioritization, coaching, and strategic clarity. The sooner you redefine your value around outcomes and enablement rather than output, the easier it becomes to release control. Key question: “Are you holding on to certain tasks because they’re critical to the business or because they’re tied to how you see yourself?” Delegate ownership, not just tasks Delegation often fails because managers hand off pieces of work but keep decision-making authority. That creates bottlenecks and sends a subtle message that you don’t fully trust your team. So try to focus on transferring ownership, not just tasks. When you give away a project, clarify the outcome, align on guardrails, and then give your employee space to determine the “how.” This doesn’t mean disappearing; it means shifting into a coaching role. Regular check-ins should center on progress, learning, and obstacles, not on rewriting their work to match how you would have done it. When ownership is real, employees step up differently. They don’t just complete assignments; they take responsibility for results. And you might even find that you like their way better. Key question: “When you delegate, are you truly giving decision-making authority or just assigning more to-do items?” Use AI to elevate AI tools now handle drafting, summarizing, reporting, and organizing at remarkable speed. But don’t fall into the trap of using AI to do more work yourself instead of expanding your team’s scope. Instead, start by automating low-value tasks with AI. Then, redistribute the higher-value task to the right owner. Ask yourself questions like: If AI drafts the first version of a report, who can interpret the insights? If AI summarizes customer feedback, who can shape the strategy? Remember: AI should create space for your team to operate at a more strategic level, not create bottlenecks by generating more outputs for you to approve. Key question: “Is AI helping you empower your team, or simply making it easier for you to keep doing everything yourself?” Build safety around mistakes Letting go is risky. When someone else owns a piece of your former work, they might approach it differently. They might even fail. If the environment doesn’t allow for that, you might instinctively take control back. This is one of many scenarios in which building psychological safety can help. Do your best to normalize experimentation and treat mistakes as learning moments rather than proof that delegation was a bad idea. At the end of a project, debrief on what worked, what didn’t, and what can be improved next time. This builds resilience and reduces the temptation to reclaim your “Legos” at the first sign of friction. Key question: “Does your team feel safe enough to try, stumble, and grow without you stepping back in to take over?” Continue to protect your time Ultimately, giving away ownership as a leader isn’t about doing less; it’s about doing differently. Your highest leverage comes from clarifying strategy, strengthening cross-functional alignment, developing talent, and anticipating what’s next. If your calendar is still dominated by executional tasks, it’s a signal that something hasn’t shifted. Think about conducting a time audit to identify which activities require your unique perspective and which could be owned by someone else. Specifically protect space for thinking, coaching, and long-term planning. Letting go creates capacity, not emptiness. And that capacity is where real leadership lives. Key question: “Are you spending most of your time building small towers - or designing the blueprint for the next big one?” Water cooler chatter A Stanford matchmaking algorithm has taken over campus. More than 5,000 Stanford students have used a platform called ‘Date Drop’ that answers 66 questions about their values and lifestyles, then pairs compatible students with matches that "drop" every Tuesday, according to The Wall Street Journal. The application just raised $2.1 million in venture-capital funding and has spread to 10 other colleges including Columbia, Princeton, and MIT. "A lot of people at Stanford place so much emphasis on success in other areas aside from social interaction, so it just naturally falls to the wayside." Alena Zhang, Stanford sophomore Businesses cashed in on heartbreak this Valentine's Day. Zoos let scorned lovers name animal dung after their exes for $5, while rage rooms host sold-out "table-flipping" experiences. Google searches for "I hate Valentine's Day" surged over 5,000% this past month, according to Google data. "Even for those who aren't currently heartbroken, participating in 'anti-Valentine's Day' events can serve as a small act of self-affirmation - a way to acknowledge past challenges, celebrate resilience, and perhaps even laugh at what once hurt." Raluca Ursu, Associate Professor of Marketing, NYU Stern School of Business Question of the week Last week’s answer: 70% This week’s question: What percent of workers globally say their commute is a time suck? Title: Are you reading Medium’s best stories?
Body: Medium. ( https://medium.com?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow ) Make the most of your time on Medium. ( https://medium.com/@MediumStaff/list/staff-picks-c7bc6e1ee00f?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ) Hi Dksgnsrb, There’s a lot to discover on Medium: writers from all over the world, publications devoted to any conceivable subject, and stories of every type. While your Feed and Daily Digest are shaped by your chosen topics, reading history, and engagement, there are a few other things you can do to get even more from your Medium experience. *01.* ( https://medium.com/me/following/suggestions?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ) *Follow wisely (and widely).* ( https://medium.com/me/following/suggestions?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ) Choose some of your suggested writers, topics, and publications ( https://medium.com/me/following/suggestions?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ) to follow. This will help populate your feed with some familiar voices and subjects you already care about. *02.* ( https://medium.com/@MediumStaff/list/staff-picks-c7bc6e1ee00f?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ) *Keep up with our staff’s favorites.* ( https://medium.com/@MediumStaff/list/staff-picks-c7bc6e1ee00f?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ) Be sure to save our staff picks ( https://medium.com/@MediumStaff/list/staff-picks-c7bc6e1ee00f?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ) to Your Library. These stories are hand-selected by members of our curation team for quality and span subject matter. *03.* ( https://medium.com/@dailynewsletter?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ) *Give your week a new highlight.* ( https://medium.com/@dailynewsletter?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ) Subscribe to our Newsletter ( https://medium.com/@dailynewsletter?source=mktgemail-9d22c184c340--follow-------------------------088ab79e_0d84_11f1_8ee4_1afda6f91a36 ). Published each Friday, it combines a thoughtful essay with a collection of links to notable stories from around the Medium universe. That’s all for now. Enjoy your reading time. Title: Thank you for registering with Gootickets
Body:
Dear Sir / Madam,

Thank you for registering with Gootickets.com!

We are excited to welcome you to the Gootickets community and look forward to providing you with many amazing sport experiences.

Your account email address:

dksgnsrb@gmail.com

To login to your account, use the address above and provide the password you used when creating your account.

As a Gootickets customer, you will experience these great benefits:

  • Superior customer service over phone and email
  • Unbeatable prices in over 40 sports events across the world
  • The finest choice of tickets with different delivery methods
  • Access to a secure site with a choice of payment methods
  • Secured delivery with DHL worldwide for printed tickets
  • A wide range of hospitality services to complete your experience
  • Access to Hotels for every destination close to your event

    Your account is now active and you can already place orders on our website.

Remember that you can access your account at any time to:

  • View and download invoices
  • Add Delivery Addresses for future orders
  • Download e-tickets and e-vouchers for selected events
  • Edit your personal information

Tip: keep this email for future reference as it may be useful when trying to login to your account or contacting us.

Need to talk to us?
Our offices are open Monday to Friday, 09:00 to 18:00 (Central European Time – GMT 1)
info@gootickets.com / +34 931225754 / +1 (646) 760-8347

Best,

Your Gootickets Team.

Title: 에어비앤비 약관 및 개인정보 처리방침 개정 안내
Body: 개정된 약관은 2026년 4월 20일부터 적용됩니다. ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ %opentrack% https://www.airbnb.co.kr/?src_section=12789870238393&c=.pi80.pkb21uaS8xLzMwNjI4NzQzNDI3Njgz&euid=92ee2474-d501-7d9b-07c5-45c5f1559d38 에어비앤비 약관 및 개⁠인⁠정⁠보 처⁠리⁠방⁠침⁠이 개⁠정⁠됩⁠니⁠다 에어비앤비 이용 약관, 결제 서비스 약관, 기타 약관과 정책(통칭하여 '약관') 및 개인정보 처리방침이 개정됩니다. 이번 개정은 변화를 거듭하고 있는 에어비앤비 비즈니스와 새로운 기능을 지원하고, 개인정보 처리 방식에 관한 정확한 정보를 추가로 제공하며, 시행이 예고된 법규를 준수하기 위한 것입니다. 기존 사용자는 2026년 4월 20일부터 개정된 약관 및 개인정보 처리방침의 적용을 받습니다. 이 날짜 이후에는 개정된 약관에 동의하고 개인정보 처리방침을 확인한 후에만 예약을 하거나 예약을 관리하실 수 있습니다. 이 날짜부터는 에어비앤비 플랫폼 이용 시 개정된 개인정보 처리방침이 적용됩니다. 개정된 약관 전문을 읽어보시기 바라며, 문서로 내려받기 [https://www.airbnb.co.kr/e/termsofserviceupdate?src_section=12237270354502&c=.pi80.pkb21uaS8xLzMwNjI4NzQzNDI3Njgz&euid=92ee2474-d501-7d9b-07c5-45c5f1559d38]도 가능하니 이용에 참고해 주세요. 개정된 약관 및 개인정보 처리방침 확인하기 * 이용 약관 [https://www.airbnb.co.kr/help/article/2854?src_section=12132576565972&c=.pi80.pkb21uaS8xLzMwNjI4NzQzNDI3Njgz&euid=92ee2474-d501-7d9b-07c5-45c5f1559d38] * 개인정보 처리방침 [https://www.airbnb.co.kr/help/article/2855?src_section=12132576565972&c=.pi80.pkb21uaS8xLzMwNjI4NzQzNDI3Njgz&euid=92ee2474-d501-7d9b-07c5-45c5f1559d38] * 결제 서비스 약관 [https://www.airbnb.co.kr/help/article/2856?src_section=12132576565972&c=.pi80.pkb21uaS8xLzMwNjI4NzQzNDI3Njgz&euid=92ee2474-d501-7d9b-07c5-45c5f1559d38] * 호스트 손해보상 [https://www.airbnb.co.kr/help/article/2869?src_section=12132576565972&c=.pi80.pkb21uaS8xLzMwNjI4NzQzNDI3Njgz&euid=92ee2474-d501-7d9b-07c5-45c5f1559d38] 또한 도움말 센터 [https://www.airbnb.co.kr/help/article/2877?src_section=12884313068572&c=.pi80.pkb21uaS8xLzMwNjI4NzQzNDI3Njgz&euid=92ee2474-d501-7d9b-07c5-45c5f1559d38]에 약관 및 개인정보 처리방침 개정 사항을 간략하게 정리해 놓았습니다. 개정된 약관 또는 개인정보 처리방침에 동의하지 않는 경우, 계정을 삭제하여 언제든지 에어비앤비와의 계약을 해지하실 수 있습니다. 에어비앤비 커뮤니티의 소중한 일원으로 함께해 주셔서 감사합니다. Airbnb Ireland UC 8 Hanover Quay Dublin 2, Ireland

Title: Growing skilled storytellers
Body: Ditching exec playbooks ᐧ Office beehives ᐧ AI doctors - February 17, 2026 monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/zz7t_7YQSbP8HmRep_d5vQ~~/AAQRxRA~/jct0xnEagxc9rDY2xDipdEypk9Sf2Phyj6S61pLKrv5aAyBEmNbmqSZ9NMidfEufr4-TXi98w0d_m10ZsKYMqnHeDbYWqnZsy0HXtLbeP3kANgytixaRGhJW_yPl-ksAbPcfWOEfQC7ECnlNOd04Bu7N4C_lrKprNQHLfl5Ha0qbHDWJmhY2OQcCCt4GYqZcyzX4wpuh2I108tyfSDL5amcG8wHsx3ypEMPkso0SbbI~ ) ’s take on the latest work trends - sent on Tuesdays. Inside this issue * Workplace trends * The AI corner * Growing skilled storytellers * Question of the week * Just for laughs * Follow the monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/mA1weORqgqwAMV0UatRZFA~~/AAQRxRA~/jct0xnEagxc9rDY2xDipdEypk9Sf2Phyj6S61pLKrv5aAyBEmNbmqSZ9NMidfEufr4-TXi98w0d_m10ZsKYMqnHeDbYWqnZsy0HXtLbeP3kANgytixaRGhJW_yPl-ksAbPcfWOEfQC7ECnlNOd04BsbxMicQ8uzFGmkmILj_7oYNzlxXUbGwoU7z0CTVCN2YT7WfR5MFRwoXB2Qpy_BeZK8zS5Wq3iSxVA6bBXpmpnc~ ) weekly on LinkedIn ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/UbthnQU4NL8DJ6e2l00VGg~~/AAQRxRA~/ryHQbvqR_ZpOmQqBf0oXtgYL5ec6SNP_lnk76wyjovX9vU9zcIVXjb-EEhCa6kwr8nbwbV3gwsFe1Vjozbg0wkgXxE1TgeTo0YB-Zhjpr431oDbcp-RZ7LhhwaS3wg4AgdUMcB2ra6y0hB2TlMrf_Ms-Knh7mLW69gCl82J_rdY~ ) Workplace trends Leadership CEOs are ditching the corporate playbook More CEOs are rejecting rigid corporate norms and running companies on their own terms. Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky no longer uses email and bans all meetings before 10 a.m. because he works best late at night, according to Fortune. His advice to other leaders is simple: don't apologize for how you want to run your company. The philosophy is spreading. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang scrapped one-on-one meetings entirely, while Whole Foods CEO Jason Buechel uses all his paid time off each year. As leaders write their own rules, the message is clear: ditching the traditional playbook might be the path to sustainable success. Wellness Office beehives become a workplace wellness trend UK employers are installing beehives on rooftops and car parks to ease workplace stress and build community, according to The Guardian. Professional beekeeping providers report surging demand as companies use beekeeping to reconnect workers with nature. One provider now serves 24 UK clients and over 10 international ones. Workers suit up with 10,000 bees flying around them during lunch breaks. Employees have expressed that the environment creates a shared sense of purpose and a physical break from the daily grind, and that typical perks can't match. The AI corner Infrastructure Big Tech's historic AI spending spree Four tech giants are planning to spend up to $670 billion on AI infrastructure in 2026 alone, the Wall Street Journal reports. Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet's combined spending represents 2.1% of US GDP - surpassing the Apollo moon program and the US interstate highway system build-out. Meta's AI costs could exceed 50% of its annual revenue for the first time, while Amazon plans to boost capital spending by nearly 60% to $200 billion this year, according to analyst projections. While Meta's investors approved the spending following the company's strong AI-driven earnings throughout 2025, Amazon lost $124 billion in market value when it announced its plans. As companies pour unprecedented resources into AI infrastructure, the investment represents one of the largest capital projects in corporate history. Healthcare AI chatbots are becoming dangerous doctors Nearly 30% of British respondents now get medical advice from AI chatbots, with 40 million people worldwide using ChatGPT for health guidance, according to The Economist. Medical experts warn that patients are arriving at doctors’ appointments armed with hours of AI-generated advice and bogus test results, forcing doctors to waste time ruling out nonexistent diseases. As AI learns from unreliable internet sources and tailors advice to anxious users, journalist and physician Deborah Cohen warns it's creating a feedback loop where worried patients receive increasingly personalized reasons to worry - making it nearly impossible to distinguish legitimate health concerns from AI-amplified anxiety, according to The Economist. Growing skilled storytellers By monday.com ( https://clicks.mail.monday.com/f/a/pDWDVwu8KrW1lgZJnfWO_w~~/AAQRxRA~/jct0xnEagxc9rDY2xDipdEypk9Sf2Phyj6S61pLKrv5aAyBEmNbmqSZ9NMidfEufr4-TXi98w0d_m10ZsKYMqnHeDbYWqnZsy0HXtLbeP3kANgytixaRGhJW_yPl-ksAbPcfWOEfQC7ECnlNOd04Buu-xOXItpgtLEDcE9xC3lGMJ6YmCGpB0jS96eOWGKgdjg_a1L2eeA7a2IFH5yOrK35p3SOegQv1jEduA-RZmJY~ ) Storytelling isn’t just for marketing — it’s a skill that can elevate every single department, and a mindset that can drive the company’s most ambitious strategies. Especially in the age of AI. Across teams, storytelling helps turn big, abstract goals into narratives that stakeholders can understand, align around, and act on. This is true whether you’re on a product team defining real-life customer pain points and how your solution can make a difference, a finance team using narratives to frame trade-offs and priorities, or an HR team telling stories that connect people to company culture and drive organizational change. In short, the human ability to add context, empathy, and perspective is what makes messages resonate. And leaders who help their employees build storytelling skills don’t just improve communication — they strengthen alignment, influence, and cross-functional collaboration. When more people can clearly articulate the impact of their work, strategy becomes visible, and momentum can grow across an entire department or company. So how can you help your team become powerhouse storytellers? Start with story significance Many employees are used to describing the work they’ve done, but not why it mattered. Maybe you’ve seen a team share a list of accomplished tasks, milestones, or metrics, but skip the part about how it supports the company’s goals. Storytelling can make a major impact on these reports by rooting them in significance to the company. The next time your team prepares a presentation, encourage them to answer questions like: What problem are we solving? Who does it impact? What would have happened if the work hadn’t been done? When your employees can anchor their communication in significance, their message becomes more memorable and easier to follow. Over time, this habit helps your team see their work as part of a larger narrative, not just a to-do list. Key question: “Do your employees clearly understand and articulate why their work matters beyond the task itself?” Build an emotionally-driven narrative structure Strong stories have movement. And they’re even more powerful when emotion is part of the journey. Something was true, something changed, and now things are different. The missing piece in many workplace updates is how that change felt or why it mattered to real people. Without that middle step, communication jumps from activity to outcome, leaving listeners unclear on how the result was achieved or why it made an impact. You can help your team tell better stories by introducing a simple structure they can use regularly: define the situation, the challenge, the action taken, and the impact. Encourage them to highlight the human side. Perhaps a product improvement reduced the time users spent on a task, allowing them to focus more on strategic work. Or maybe a well-planned marketing initiative made sales conversations smoother, leading to more big wins. This kind of storytelling engages and moves an audience, leading to stronger alignment, buy-in, and action. Key question: “Can your employees explain their work as a clear before-during-and-after story?” Build audience awareness One reason storytelling is so powerful is that it centers on the listener, not just the speaker. A great story meets the audience where they are and connects the message to what they care about. With this in mind, try to encourage your team to think about who will hear or read their update before they craft it. Consider questions like: What does that audience already know? What are they worried about? What decisions do they need to make? From here, your employees can tailor their presentations to specific stakeholders – be it their peers, leadership, or customers – and adjust their pitch to those perspectives. This mindset shift, from reporting information to fostering understanding, dramatically improves communication and deepens connection to the material. Key question: “How can your employees tell stories that audiences see themselves in?” Make reflection part of the process Stories don’t only live in big presentations; they’re also how people make sense of their own progress. After a project wraps or a milestone is reached, include moments for reflection in your retros. Ask your team what surprised them, what obstacles they faced, and what they’d do differently next time. These conversations help employees move beyond surface-level reporting and start identifying the turning points and lessons that make stories meaningful. You might even record both the presentation and the retrospective meeting, then run both transcripts through AI and ask for story-improvement tips, such as which parts of the story could be clearer, where more emotional context is needed, or how to make the ending more impactful. Key question: “Do you give your employees space to reflect on their projects?” Encourage low-stakes storytelling practice For many people, storytelling feels intimidating because they associate it with high-pressure moments, like executive presentations. You want to build a storytelling muscle in your employees, readying them for any situation, so try giving them regular, low-stakes practice and opportunities. This might mean starting a team meeting with a quick “win story,” inviting team members to explain how they solved a tricky problem, or even a story of an initiative that failed, grounded in humor, resilience, and learnings for next time. These informal reps help storytelling feel like a normal part of work, not a special event. As confidence grows, employees become more comfortable bringing stories into bigger settings. Key question: “Where can you create more opportunities for your team to practice sharing the story behind their work?” Water cooler chatter Apple's call-screening feature gives everyone a virtual assistant to filter calls. Launched as part of iOS 26, the tool asks unknown callers their name and reason for calling before you pick up. It's tackling America's spam problem - over 2 billion robocalls monthly - but frustrating those who expect immediate answers. "Calling people you don't know on the phone in 2026 is as bad as randomly knocking on a friend's door unannounced in 1995." Jason Calacanis, Angel Investor and Podcast Host Defense tech startup Anduril ditch résumés for an AI drone racing competition. The $30 billion company invites anyone to prove their coding skills by building autonomous racing algorithms - no degree needed. Winners split $500,000, and the top performer skips straight to job interviews, says Fortune. "When I hire people at Anduril, I look for people who have done projects that were outside of what their work paid them to do or what their school made them do." Palmer Luckey, Founder of Anduril Question of the week Last week’s answer: 45% This week’s question: What is the global average percentage of workers who say they have a good work-life balance? Title: [SW Expert Academy] 휴면 계정 안내
Body: 휴면 계정 전환 안내 - SW Expert Academy 휴면 계정 전환 안내 안녕하세요. SW Expert Academy입니다. 회원님의 개인정보보호를 위해 1년 이상 SW Expert Academy 에 로그인하지 않은 계정 은 정보통신망 이용 촉진 및 정보보호 등에 관한 법률에 따라 휴면 계정으로 전환될 예정입니다. 휴면 계정으로 전환된 이후에는 회원님의 개인정보가 분리 보관됨에 따라 SW Expert Academy가 제공하는 안내 서비스를 제공받으실 수 없습니다. 휴면 계정 처리를 원하지 않으실 경우 SW Expert Academy에 아이디와 비밀번호로 로그인을 해주시기 바랍니다. 휴면전환 처리대상 계정 1년 이상 서비스 이용이 없는 회원 휴면전환 예정일 2026년 03월 17일 분리보관 정보 회원정보 및 서비스 신청시 입력한 개인정보 ※ 상시 SW 역량테스트의 경우 유효기간 2년에 따라 휴면계정 전환 후 2년간 저장/관리 처리 방법 휴면 계정으로 전환 및 개인정보 별도 저장 관리 SW Expert Academy 바로가기 본 메일은 발신 전용이므로 회신 되지 않습니다. 문의사항은 홈페이지의 Q&A 를 이용해 주시기 바랍니다. ⓒ 2017 SAMSUNG. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED.
